name: Jim Jordan
subject: I’m going public
body:

| ATTN: Peter,
---

# [District Round-up 01.25.26](https://public-api.wordpress.com/bar/?stat=groovemails-events&bin=wpcom_email_click&redirect_to=http%3A%2F%2Fbethfarnhamforcongress.com%2F2026%2F01%2F25%2Fdistrict-round-up-01-25-26%2F&sr=0&signature=49e1419ace7d96d3a6dc4a08dda754d1&blog_id=213069660&user=248418217&_&_z=z)
---
By **Elizabeth Farnham** on January 25, 2026
---
| Dear Neighbor,
---
Thank you for reading my District Round-up. As your candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District, it is my honor and pleasure to deliver to you where I’ve been and what issues I’ve tackled in the past week. Criss-crossing the counties of Adams, Franklin, Fulton, Bedford, Cambria, Blair, Huntingdon, Mifflin, Juniata, Perry, Cumberland, and Somerset gives me great opportunities to meet with voters, hear what is on your hearts and minds, and the chance to voice my fight for Everyday Americans. If you would like to contribute to my campaign, please donate here: [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beth-farnham-for-congress-1](https://bethfarnhamforcongress.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=25d4cd9066f6073fa9e691f1cef5c66a8363d5b88521cc6122ea19edf778dac8&blog_id=213069660&post_id=6165&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=87f98ebb7bac09681dce27c57ab9f178&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYWN0Ymx1ZS5jb20vZG9uYXRlL2JldGgtZmFybmhhbS1mb3ItY29uZ3Jlc3MtMQ=&email_id=a6e13ccf88fcd7e5fc6be4d564c84d96)
---
|
---
Photo credit: museryfantastico (threads)
---
The murder of Renee Good was a chance for Immigration Customs Enforcement (ICE) to pause, re-evaluate their agenda, retrain their agents, and reassure the nation that they would not shoot unarmed civilians again. Of course, every murder, like that of Keith Porter, Jr., they had committed in the name of “securing our borders” had been the same opportunity for reform, but Ms. Good’s murder was captured on film by several witnesses was shared around the world, and received more attention than any of the others, so her execution became the springboard for change.
---
Instead, Department of Homeland Security Secretary, Krisit Noem, claimed Ms. Good committed “an act of domestic terrorism.” In attempting to clear his name, murderer Jonathan Ross released his own cell phone video which captured Ms. Good’s last words that further damned his actions, “Whatever Dude, I’m not mad at you.”
---
In other words, there has been no contrition from ICE, DHS, or the Trump administration, no pause, no preventative measures, no change whatsoever. Therefore, yesterday, just prior to my Town Hall, another unarmed civilian was gunned down in cold blood by an ICE agent.
---
This time the victim was an ICU nurse at the VA, named Alex Pretti, who was also in legal possession of a gun in his waistband. Multiple videos taken by witnesses show Mr. Pretti being pepper-sprayed, assaulted, disarmed, then executed by 10 gunshots.
---
Thus, we must abolish ICE.
---
Should we protect our borders? Sure, but this is clearly not the agency through which to do it. ICE no longer serves The People, but a tyrannical administration.
---
So please call Senators Fetterman and McCormick and demand they vote against DHS funding until ICE is abolished.
---
Because until ICE is abolished, we can expect many more people exercising their Constitutional rights to be brutally killed in broad daylight by out of control government officials who have no accountability.
---
|
---
Many thanks to all who attended the Town Hall on Healthcare and Affordability yesterday in Chambersburg. I appreciated the opportunity to speak about these important issues to Everyday Americans. The One Big Beautiful Bill Act is another form of harm that Republicans are inflicting on the American people and it should be countered by a newly-elected Congress in 2026 of which I plan to be a part of. Pictures to come.
---
| | |
---|---|---
|
---|---
Many, many thanks to the Franklin County Coalition for Progress and to the Central Food Bank of Harrisburg for providing opportunities to Beth Farnham for Congress to serve the community.
---
Team Beth Farnham for Congress has been hard at work cleaning the offices for the Franklin County Housing Authority and packing boxes of food for hungry Pennsylvanians. I am grateful to Sandy, Brian, Jack, and all of the team for all that they do for others.
---
Together we [#ChooseDemocracy](https://bethfarnhamforcongress.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=4935ba4de6c23edd799c0e040944b776cdf47567a202167f9366d3b51af322eb&blog_id=213069660&post_id=6165&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=bb0269a728214b5d7dfef013d1dbc1a6&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=a6e13ccf88fcd7e5fc6be4d564c84d96) and Community!
---
Many thanks to PCN Select for releasing their video of my speech at the Penn Ag Dem luncheon on January 10th.
---

clean_body: Dear Neighbor, Thank you for reading my District Round-up. As your candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District, it is my honor and pleasure to deliver to you where I’ve been... | | | | | or ---|---|--- By Elizabeth Farnham on January 25, 2026 | Dear Neighbor, Thank you for reading my District Round-up. As your candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District, it is my honor and pleasure to deliver to you where I’ve been and what issues I’ve tackled in the past week. Criss-crossing the counties of Adams, Franklin, Fulton, Bedford, Cambria, Blair, Huntingdon, Mifflin, Juniata, Perry, Cumberland, and Somerset gives me great opportunities to meet with voters, hear what is on your hearts and minds, and the chance to voice my fight for Everyday Americans. If you would like to contribute to my campaign, please donate here: | Photo credit: museryfantastico (threads) The murder of Renee Good was a chance for Immigration Customs Enforcement (ICE) to pause, re-evaluate their agenda, retrain their agents, and reassure the nation that they would not shoot unarmed civilians again. Of course, every murder, like that of Keith Porter, Jr., they had committed in the name of “securing our borders” had been the same opportunity for reform, but Ms. Good’s murder was captured on film by several witnesses was shared around the world, and received more attention than any of the others, so her execution became the springboard for change. Instead, Department of Homeland Security Secretary, Krisit Noem, claimed Ms. Good committed “an act of domestic terrorism.” In attempting to clear his name, murderer Jonathan Ross released his own cell phone video which captured Ms. Good’s last words that further damned his actions, “Whatever Dude, I’m not mad at you.” In other words, there has been no contrition from ICE, DHS, or the Trump administration, no pause, no preventative measures, no change whatsoever. Therefore, yesterday, just prior to my Town Hall, another unarmed civilian was gunned down in cold blood by an ICE agent. This time the victim was an ICU nurse at the VA, named Alex Pretti, who was also in legal possession of a gun in his waistband. Multiple videos taken by witnesses show Mr. Pretti being pepper-sprayed, assaulted, disarmed, then executed by 10 gunshots. Thus, we must abolish ICE. Should we protect our borders? Sure, but this is clearly not the agency through which to do it. ICE no longer serves The People, but a tyrannical administration. So please call Senators Fetterman and McCormick and demand they vote against DHS funding until ICE is abolished. Because until ICE is abolished, we can expect many more people exercising their Constitutional rights to be brutally killed in broad daylight by out of control government officials who have no accountability. | Many thanks to all who attended the Town Hall on Healthcare and Affordability yesterday in Chambersburg. I appreciated the opportunity to speak about these important issues to Everyday Americans. The One Big Beautiful Bill Act is another form of harm that Republicans are inflicting on the American people and it should be countered by a newly-elected Congress in 2026 of which I plan to be a part of. Pictures to come. | | | ---|---|--- | ---|--- Many, many thanks to the Franklin County Coalition for Progress and to the Central Food Bank of Harrisburg for providing opportunities to Beth Farnham for Congress to serve the community. Team Beth Farnham for Congress has been hard at work cleaning the offices for the Franklin County Housing Authority and packing boxes of food for hungry Pennsylvanians. I am grateful to Sandy, Brian, Jack, and all of the team for all that they do for others. Together we and Community! Many thanks to PCN Select for releasing their video of my speech at the Penn Ag Dem luncheon on January 10th. If you’re like me, you love this country as much as I do and work to make a positive difference. In the midst of this Constitutional Crisis, please consider reaching out to your local Democratic committees or Democratic candidates so that we can strengthen our Democracy instead of letting it slide into fascism. We don’t just #ChooseDemocracy, we choose Pennsylvania, we choose The United States of America, we choose Humanity and I am deeply glad to work along side you. Sincerely, Beth | | | | Beth Farnham for Congress © 2026. or . | | | | Get the Jetpack app Subscribe, bookmark, and get real-time notifications – all from one app! | | | | ---|--- | | | | Automattic, Inc. 60 29th St. #343, San Francisco, CA 94110

I'm reaching out to you from the **Sound of Freedom Foundation** -- the organization that funds real-time rescue operations in partnership with law enforcement and former special forces to find and extract children from traffickers -- fast.When a tip comes in, the teams made up of former Navy SEALs, undercover investigators, and cyber-intelligence analysts move within hours, sometimes minutes.In partnership with local law enforcement, we pull these children out of hotels, vehicles moving across the country, and brothels.The **Sound of Freedom Foundation** team works tirelessly to protect youth from the ravages of human trafficking and exploitation. Our mission is clear - to create a world where no child ever has to live in fear of being trafficked or exploited. We don't wait.Today -- a child is waiting to be found.I'm personally asking you: will you help me rescue one before midnight?

* Most victims are only 12 years old
* 35-40 million suffer in sex trafficking worldwide every single day
* Less than 0.04% are ever found - but we can change that

We can't continue to stand by and let these children live in fear and terror.Your gift today goes straight to the next rescue:

* $25 keeps our 24/7 rescue hotline open one more shift
* $50 provides transportation to a safe house
* $100 gives one rescued child 72 hours in a safe house with a warm bed, food, and someone to hold them while they cry

I wish I could do this alone, but I can't.I need you with me today. Please don't close this email without helping. One child's future -- one little boy or girl who still believes someone is coming -- is hanging on what we do in the next few hours.Click below to give today - Every single dollar lands instantly where seconds save lives.

name: SDC HQ
subject: 2026 is NOT the most important election of our lifetimes.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats
body:
please let us explain...
#
You won’t see this message from anyone else in your inbox today:
**2026 is NOT the most important election of our lifetimes.**
That might be surprising to hear. But it’s the truth, Peter...
**The most important election will happen in 2028 – when we will restore our democracy by electing a Democrat to lead our country.**
But let’s be clear: _That will not be possible if we don’t build momentum and support strong Democrats up and down the ballot in 2026._
**Winning BIG this year will set the stage for what’s to come.** And Peter, today we’re asking for your help to do exactly that.
**[Will you support Democrats running in competitive races across the United States by chipping in $15 or whatever works best for you today? name: Voter Action Project Team
subject: Their masks are off
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Every suppressive action these vengeful partisans take shows their true colors. For all their talk about election integrity, voter ID, and not harming eligible voters, closing legitimate polling places to make it harder to vote only has one logical end result.
Attacks by opponents of voting rights on young voters, mail-in ballots, early voting, and more aren't about integrity and fairness. It's about protecting their own power at any cost and tilting the playing field before the game has even been played.
We must stand up to stop them at every turn. RUSH $35 or more today to help us fight back in North Carolina and beyond >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=012526_2&amount=35]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
NC Newsline: Amid protests, NC elections board rejects campus voting sites and Sunday voting in several counties [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=012526_2&amount=25]
We're now facing the repercussions of the partisan election takeover laws that went into effect at the end of 2024, Team.
To refresh your memory: After the 2024 elections and the defeat of a MAGA candidate for governor, opponents of voting rights in North Carolina jammed through a bill that took the power of the governor to appoint people to election boards and handed that to the state auditor . It's worth noting that the state auditor is the only statewide elected Republican, with Democrats winning the 2024 races for governor, lieutenant governor, attorney general, and secretary of state.
Now, among massive protests from young voters, the MAGA-fied North Carolina State Elections Board recently shot down proposals to continue having early voting sites on several universities around the state . These weren't new sites either; these were places where North Carolina had provided early voting locations for a decade or more.
This is voter suppression, plain and simple, by making it harder for eligible, registered voters to vote in-person by taking away their polling place. It's another demonstration of how opponents of voting rights in North Carolina are more focused on expanding and cementing their own power than they are in serving the people and having fair elections.
We have to fight these suppressive actions in North Carolina and any other state where opponents of voting rights try to replicate this. Step up with $35 or whatever you can today to help every eligible voter cast their ballot >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=012526_2&amount=35]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=012526_2&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=012526_2&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=012526_2&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=012526_2&amount=100] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=012526_2&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=012526_2&amount=35]
These new, highly partisan State Election Board members wouldn't even look the protestors in the eye or engage with their proposals. After the vote, Chairman Francis De Luca threatened to call the cops on students if they didn’t leave.
Need to better understand how this impacts young voters? Western Carolina University in Jackson County, for example, has had a polling place on campus since 2016 that’s been used in five general elections and four primaries, according to a document prepared for the board. The state board voted 3-2 to get rid of it.
Closing the campus voting site would not save money, but “ would create barriers to voting for young, rural residents ,” Jackson County Board member Betsy Swift told the state board. 64% of students don’t have vehicles to drive to the polling place, she said.
Without the on-campus site, the closest polling place for students will be a recreation center nearly two miles away. Students without transportation will have to walk along a four-lane highway and through a meadow to get there.
Voter suppression isn't just black and white photos of eligible voters being turned away from polling places. Voter suppression is making it harder for eligible voters to take part in our elections through onerous ID requirements, closing polling places, taking away voting locations, or ending mail-in voting. Every time someone makes it harder to cast your ballot, voters are suppressed.
It's not going to end until we stop them.
Opponents of voting rights aren't going to stop attacking our fundamental right to vote until we stop them at the ballot box and take away their power. Chip in $35 or whatever you can today to help fight back nationwide >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=012526_2&amount=35]
https://voteractionproject.org/ [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=012526_2&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=012526_2&amount=20]
Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code.

# [They shot a nurse - then called him a terrorist](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=185788781&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1Nzg4NzgxLCJpYXQiOjE3NjkzOTA5MDgsImV4cCI6MTc3MTk4MjkwOCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Hxfeoj_JLZuSXWYDk10Qkt2FTFdf7mTH8PXThlLFIKU)
### When the Constitution Falls into Ordinary Hands
| | [Melissa Chaudhry](https://substack.com/@melissa4congress)
---
| Jan 26
---
| [](https://substack.com/@melissa4congress)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=185788781&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1Nzg4NzgxLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2OTM5MDkwOCwiZXhwIjoxNzcxOTgyOTA4LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzI2MDExMSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.DP5v5jWP3ALOEAWgRzmdW98QViV-_qGrfiALAoDH_Pc&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=185788781&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1Nzg4NzgxLCJpYXQiOjE3NjkzOTA5MDgsImV4cCI6MTc3MTk4MjkwOCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Hxfeoj_JLZuSXWYDk10Qkt2FTFdf7mTH8PXThlLFIKU&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=185788781&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1Nzg4NzgxLCJpYXQiOjE3NjkzOTA5MDgsImV4cCI6MTc3MTk4MjkwOCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Hxfeoj_JLZuSXWYDk10Qkt2FTFdf7mTH8PXThlLFIKU)
---
| | [](https://substack..rMvK-gbnZrakatYcoH4U475YERbipKLH2EayjRw0RW0?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/melissa4congress/p/they-shot-a-nurse-then-called-him?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Yesterday in Minneapolis, federal immigration agents shot and killed **Alex Pretti** , a 37-year-old ICU nurse at the Minneapolis VA Health Care System.
He wasn't a threat. He was the person who _held veterans ' hands as they died_. Colleagues, friends, and family describe him as a steady, compassionate caregiver who showed up because he believed human beings are still owed dignity--especially when the state is at its most forceful.
And then came the secondary violence: within moments, senior officials used the microphone to launder the killing--to frame the victim as the danger. DHS and White House figures labeled Pretti a "domestic terrorist" before any transparent investigation could even begin.
This is exactly why due process matters: when the state kills, **the public is owed a clean chain of evidence, an independent investigation,** and**accountability that is not written by the same agency that pulled the trigger**. A federal judge has reportedly ordered DHS to preserve evidence in the case - because they, reportedly, would rather destroy it.
### The bigger point (and it's old as empire)
When power tries to rule by fear, it must keep itself afraid--because it cannot survive in daylight.
So it lies in two directions at once:
* It tells the public to fear each other.
* It tells the public it is _afraid of us_ --so it can justify anything it does to us.

That is the logic of authoritarianism: **obedience is "safety," conscience is "terrorism," and mercy is treated as a threat.** When someone reaches down to help a fallen person, the regime can't read that as decency--because decency is not part of its operating system. So it rewrites decency as danger, and calls its own violence "order."
This is why words like "domestic terrorist" get thrown around so casually: not because they describe reality, but because they _manufacture permission._
### What we do with grief: empathy, humanity, memory
**Empathy** is not softness. _It is clarity about what a human being is._
**Humanity** is not naivete. _It is refusal to become the thing that is hunting us._
**Memory** is not nostalgia. _It is the record that stops power from editing the past into a weapon._
If you want a practical place to stand in a moment like this, start here:
1. **Tell the truth, and separate what is confirmed from what is alleged.**
2. **Insist on an investigation the agency cannot control.**
3. **Refuse the moral inversion** where caregivers become enemies and uniforms become innocence.

A republic is not proven by its slogans but by its restraint--especially when fear is profitable and violence is easy. When the state kills and then smears the dead, it is not defending order; it is defending impunity. Our duty is to hold fast to the human record--empathy, humanity, memory--until law becomes real again.
### If you want to support my work (and keep the record alive)
My book, _**Service & Sacrifice**_, is not a "personal story." It's a field manual for the era we're in: what happens when systems are captured, truth is treated as a threat, and due process survives only when ordinary people insist on it--calmly, relentlessly, on the record.
We lived it. We fought it. And we **beat Pam Bondi 's machine in court**.
If institutions won't hold the Constitution, the duty doesn't vanish--it **transfers**. To you. To me. To anyone who refuses the moral inversion that calls decency "danger."
* Order on [Amazon](https://substack.com/redirect/339983e5-1b8d-4198-a141-266a66bba443?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) or [Metalabel](https://substack.com/redirect/ffe7143a-d767-4f4c-9bb3-bef9dee335d2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* Review on [Amazon](https://substack.com/redirect/305b6747-f30b-4c31-95a1-72152519e950?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (reviews are oxygen for a new book)
* Share with someone still tempted to believe it's all going to be ok.

And join me **live** with [Qasim Rashid](https://substack.com/redirect/189649a8-79a1-4caf-8629-721dd2775321?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Tue Jan 27 at 2:00pm PT**. We'll talk about moral and civic orientation when the guardrails are gone--and what disciplined, nonviolent integrity looks like now.
Til then, stay strong,
Melissa

name: Tobias Read
subject: Pam Bondi just crossed a line.
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=260125em-bondi-pet [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=260125em-bondi-pet]
Pam Bondi just crossed a line.
She threatened Minnesota with continued federal occupation and lawlessness at the hands of ICE agents — unless the state hands over its voter files.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=260125em-bondi-pet [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=260125em-bondi-pet]
This is coercion, plain and simple. And it sets a dangerous precedent for every state in the country.
Election systems should never be treated as leverage. Voter data should never be demanded under threat. And states should never be pressured into surrendering their democratic infrastructure.
Oregon believes in a different approach. We believe elections belong to the people, that election workers deserve protection, and that democracy is strengthened when states stand together.
That’s why we’re speaking out now. But we need your voice in the mix, too, if we’re going to take on this lawless administration and win.
So I’m personally asking you to add your name to my grassroots petition. Together, let’s send a message that we stand with Minneapolis, reject the use of federal power to bully states, and call on federal leaders to respect the integrity of our elections. This moment matters, and we can’t afford anyone on the sidelines when the stakes are this high. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/Zxp2INlRCkGiRWxomyZ9uA2]
ADD MY NAME [https://secure.ngpvan.com/Zxp2INlRCkGiRWxomyZ9uA2]
Thanks for making your voice heard and taking a stand,
Tobias Read
CONTRIBUTE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=260125em-bondi-pet]

Portland, OR 97242

subject: I was sixteen when I lied to join the Marines.
body:
I was wounded twice, but many never made it home...
I still remember seeing the flag raised on Iwo Jima...

| | | [](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=41109c33ba294824831a806b4baf2ffc&_e=PV3OZ6SvMstRxM_xxXRquXuKa8jrbuY88e9DxljctQ_L6SAUI1QHxMWAvd3RD71fsi8kWmQezTaTzNbD5z8lN7zT6AWjJ3dIKSVBIfg7krwr4PN7TP4Y0Wgbpxjgll013NDvkDGhPK9AqZfGtJNC98vnhBJxZPk3hEovpinug1jnEvFS88jL1qG33Wqqm_ajBXJoKPT6P5prOXvhKQX_ATgYB3Tb5Mnji6_F1nkaFWmONqRCjGxSuQUDdRIzBvKXPZLq36r7MzEQ10DKTLliV95YgZUt13vwrD7x0eIWDmek78c0BtNi2KJiiuv9KsaX6-95avmvrro7Dt8IWsm4C1PL-B8NwpZcpjyETpcoL_ByUcQBxZQjVZ-FshFMrcXJANOP8hUXU6Ra60rHQU4PRvtt0BNr7KnLDrllm2Qdm31Ls4Q00RO3EzLQ3tnbXN9KM_l5JdxxTxoX_9tVbrMPXgW6iw_Ay6cQwkKmBbqBWyxlRoeeDMOwos1jwMRA3cKOrpF7uEb6LSGZ72-Wy2XTLEXXvwi9-xbP48al3eW9cQlpBNGUZd75aXbVZCmWy06EHwGXp69aLJjCDwWcDmI2XGtLUF6uIBloItRbUMjDpmGOhbJm47OhE86Fd-cRQgw0WJVNel4pj3ym_Dex5z7m6xUFnNVyUMozWgA0nmtqj0Q%3D)

My name is **Corporal Frank S. Wright** , United States Marine Corps, retired. I'm a hundred years old now -- one of the last living Marine Raiders of World War II.In January 1942, just weeks after the attack on Pearl Harbor, I quit high school in Arkansas and walked into the enlistment office. I was sixteen -- too young, too skinny, and too light to qualify. The sergeant told me to come back when I was heavier.So I went home, ate all the bananas I could find, and stuffed five pounds' worth into my pockets before stepping on that scale again.I passed. I lied about my age. And I became a Marine.
---

---
After boot camp, I volunteered to join the **4th Marine Raiders** , America's first special operations unit.They asked me why I wanted to join. I said, _" Because I want to fight."_Our commanding officer was **James Roosevelt** , the President's son -- a good man, the kind who never gave an order he wouldn't do himself. We were trained to land first, fight behind enemy lines, and take the beaches nobody else could reach.From **Guadalcanal** to **New Georgia** , from the jungles of the Solomons to the black sand of **Iwo Jima** , we fought to free the Pacific.On **Guam** , I was stabbed in the stomach during hand-to-hand combat. On **Iwo Jima** , I was hit by machine-gun fire and knocked unconscious. Both times, my men thought I was gone. Somehow, I wasn't.When I came to, I saw the flag rising on Mount Suribachi. Every ship in the harbor sounded its horn. I had tears in my eyes then, and I still do when I think about it.That flag meant freedom still lived.
---
[](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=41109c33ba294824831a806b4baf2ffc&_e=PV3OZ6SvMstRxM_xxXRquXuKa8jrbuY88e9DxljctQ_L6SAUI1QHxMWAvd3RD71fsi8kWmQezTaTzNbD5z8lN7zT6AWjJ3dIKSVBIfg7krwr4PN7TP4Y0Wgbpxjgll013NDvkDGhPK9AqZfGtJNC98vnhBJxZPk3hEovpinug1jnEvFS88jL1qG33Wqqm_ajBXJoKPT6P5prOXvhKQX_ATgYB3Tb5Mnji6_F1nkaFWl_YUhxKFXVeof-9dcpoRhG3XaPMtkoxLjwA2cSOnVB48j46O0sEL5ovGHSLKERs-vSCPn-0IY5Fb1UPkulK2qpPl8tL8aLywEUfquOSn0HIL9az65jRzEdD-zbdxiAH7uEbzWlp32DQzateEjcLYtZH9LkI588duOiXM1RXFBTSgo7SfDgQbf4OyCTcBJDylyKomVC8ivWFzsb1QU13s-lIkejoeTsvX0lTW8YQKwvg4c0Erwpn1eX8j7G5enU1IuvG-KTBp9DemOetm63g4VjbHyItTKI1bptEovO_knEuiaU-fS_5kEQlF9zTCr-4BV7_hSpsZf-WKIhDaF-gu0B4RuxnJ74jlo2UQEW2BQ_vZNv4ey_8--tmBx7gedgGon_Ocaq49seUhNTVQ0MHac8SXWUk0bH9h6aFLlFagZbxTdZVkjXgw1kzEGYXkfGe6Q%3D)
---
There were dark days too...On Guadalcanal, I caught malaria and ended up in a field hospital -- but when my unit was preparing to move out, I couldn't stand being left behind.I slipped out and rejoined them. I'll now admit, I was always a little rebellious. When the war ended, I became a drill instructor, helping shape the next generation of Marines. I was in my twenties, carrying wounds inside and out.For more than a decade, I didn't share my stories. I kept them locked away, like many of my brothers did. Over the years, I began to open up and share my story with friends, family, and others who wanted to know about the realities of the battlefield at the Pacific Front.Now, more than ever, the **American Veterans Center** has given me the opportunity to share them -- on the stage at the **Annual Conference** , and by capturing them on film for future generations. Not just my story, but those of the men who never came home, and those who are no longer here to tell theirs.
---

And every year, the **American Veterans Center Annual Conference** gives us something rare -- a gathering where history stands shoulder to shoulder with the future.It's where I've met cadets who listen with the same wide eyes I once had at sixteen. It's where old comrades find each other again, and where we all remember that the values we fought for -- courage, duty, honor -- are still being carried forward.It's a living bridge between those who fought for freedom and those who will defend it next.

But I still believe in this country -- in what it stands for, and in the people who keep its history alive.If you believe that too, I ask you to help the American Veterans Center continue its work. So that long after the last of us is gone, our voices will still be heard -- reminders of what America can be when its people stand together.Thank you for remembering. Thank you for caring. And thank you for keeping faith with those who served.**Semper Fidelis,**

**P.S.** When I was sixteen, I had to lie about my age to fight for my country. Now, at one hundred, I can tell you the truth as plain as I know it: freedom isn't free, and the stories of those who fought for it are fading fast. Your gift -- whatever you can give -- helps make sure they're never forgotten.

clean_body: I was wounded twice, but many never made it home... I still remember seeing the flag raised on Iwo Jima... | | | Peter, My name is Corporal Frank S. Wright , United States Marine Corps, retired. I'm a hundred years old now -- one of the last living Marine Raiders of World War II.In January 1942, just weeks after the attack on Pearl Harbor, I quit high school in Arkansas and walked into the enlistment office. I was sixteen -- too young, too skinny, and too light to qualify. The sergeant told me to come back when I was heavier.So I went home, ate all the bananas I could find, and stuffed five pounds' worth into my pockets before stepping on that scale again.I passed. I lied about my age. And I became a Marine. After boot camp, I volunteered to join the 4th Marine Raiders , America's first special operations unit.They asked me why I wanted to join. I said, " Because I want to fight."Our commanding officer was James Roosevelt , the President's son -- a good man, the kind who never gave an order he wouldn't do himself. We were trained to land first, fight behind enemy lines, and take the beaches nobody else could reach.From Guadalcanal to New Georgia , from the jungles of the Solomons to the black sand of Iwo Jima , we fought to free the Pacific.On Guam , I was stabbed in the stomach during hand-to-hand combat. On Iwo Jima , I was hit by machine-gun fire and knocked unconscious. Both times, my men thought I was gone. Somehow, I wasn't.When I came to, I saw the flag rising on Mount Suribachi. Every ship in the harbor sounded its horn. I had tears in my eyes then, and I still do when I think about it.That flag meant freedom still lived. There were dark days too...On Guadalcanal, I caught malaria and ended up in a field hospital -- but when my unit was preparing to move out, I couldn't stand being left behind.I slipped out and rejoined them. I'll now admit, I was always a little rebellious. When the war ended, I became a drill instructor, helping shape the next generation of Marines. I was in my twenties, carrying wounds inside and out.For more than a decade, I didn't share my stories. I kept them locked away, like many of my brothers did. Over the years, I began to open up and share my story with friends, family, and others who wanted to know about the realities of the battlefield at the Pacific Front.Now, more than ever, the American Veterans Center has given me the opportunity to share them -- on the stage at the Annual Conference , and by capturing them on film for future generations. Not just my story, but those of the men who never came home, and those who are no longer here to tell theirs. They've given old Marines like me a place to share what we lived through, to look young cadets and future service members in the eye and say, "This is what it means to serve."They've brought veterans from every generation together -- to remember, to teach, and to inspire. And every year, the American Veterans Center Annual Conference gives us something rare -- a gathering where history stands shoulder to shoulder with the future.It's where I've met cadets who listen with the same wide eyes I once had at sixteen. It's where old comrades find each other again, and where we all remember that the values we fought for -- courage, duty, honor -- are still being carried forward.It's a living bridge between those who fought for freedom and those who will defend it next. The National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C., where America gathers to honor its heroes. The Annual Veterans Conference each November, where we share stories and pass the torch to those who will serve next. The preservation of our stories through documentaries and oral histories -- so that when we are gone, the lessons remain. At my age, I don't take many things for granted. But I still believe in this country -- in what it stands for, and in the people who keep its history alive.If you believe that too, I ask you to help the American Veterans Center continue its work. So that long after the last of us is gone, our voices will still be heard -- reminders of what America can be when its people stand together.Thank you for remembering. Thank you for caring. And thank you for keeping faith with those who served.Semper Fidelis, Frank S. Wright Corporal, U.S. Marine Corps (Ret.) As part of a pro-voting rights coalition, we monitored harmful legislation, sounded the alarm, drove public engagement and advocacy campaigns, and helped make sure that those bills did not become law.
Free and fair elections are on the line — and by ensuring access to methods like voting by mail, we can help make sure more people are able to make their voices heard.

Peter,

What happened last Sunday in St. Paul should alarm every American who values faith and freedom.

A mob of far-left activists disrupted a worship service, barged into a church, and turned a sacred space into their political theater — with families and children sitting there trying to pray. That should never happen in the United States.

And it’s happening for one reason: the radical Left believes nothing is off-limits anymore. Not our laws. Not our values. Not even our churches. When people in power refuse to enforce consequences, this kind of intimidation spreads.

Then the media poured gasoline on it — defending it, amplifying it, and treating it like entertainment. Instead of drawing a line, they gave it legitimacy. That tells you exactly what we’re up against.

[**This is why holding the House in 2026 matters so much. If Democrats take power, they’ll protect the agitators, excuse the harassment, and turn a blind eye while the chaos escalates. More disruption. More intimidation. clean_body: My campaign is built on three words: Faith. Family. Future. | Peter, What happened last Sunday in St. Paul should alarm every American who values faith and freedom. A mob of far-left activists disrupted a worship service, barged into a church, and turned a sacred space into their political theater — with families and children sitting there trying to pray. That should never happen in the United States. And it’s happening for one reason: the radical Left believes nothing is off-limits anymore. Not our laws. Not our values. Not even our churches. When people in power refuse to enforce consequences, this kind of intimidation spreads. Then the media poured gasoline on it — defending it, amplifying it, and treating it like entertainment. Instead of drawing a line, they gave it legitimacy. That tells you exactly what we’re up against. My campaign is built on three words: Faith. Family. Future. I’m running because I refuse to accept a country where Christians are expected to sit down, shut up, and take it. Thank you for standing with me. For Faith, Family, and the Future, Josh Cortez | | Paid for by Josh Cortez for Congress Don't want to receive any more of our emails? We hate to see you go: .

# BLOG: Protecting Children in a Confusing Moment

# _Why Families Are Paying Attention to Amendment 3_
---
| | **Families across the country are navigating a moment of deep uncertainty when it comes to children, identity, and medical decision-making. Many parents are alarmed by how quickly some institutions are embracing permanent medical interventions for minors, often without clear boundaries or sufficient parental involvement.**
**These concerns are not abstract. Across the United States, children are now being placed on medical pathways that include...**

## About Her Health Her Future
Her Health, Her Future is the official campaign committee working to pass a Yes vote on Amendment 3 in 2026. We are a statewide coalition of pro-life families, individuals, community leaders, and organizations committed to protecting women’s health, safeguarding our children, and restoring protections for the unborn.
**Every life is worthy of protection. Join the movement today.**

name: Sara Jacobs
subject: Not another penny to DHS.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sara Jacobs for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em?amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em?amount=25]
It’s the most gut-wrenching, unspeakable deja vu.
Another person executed by federal immigration agents in Minneapolis.
Another endless stream of grainy videos, analyzing every angle of a human being’s last moments.
Another surge of lies, gaslighting, and blatant disinformation from the Trump administration as they cover up and justify a brutal murder.
It’s all so dystopian. I’m incredibly sad for every person who knew and loved Alex Pretti. And I’m angry for them, too. I’m angry for all of us.
This is not OK. This is not what democratic governments do. We can’t accept it, and we don’t have to.
It’s not fair that we’re facing this reality. We shouldn’t have to be.
But when things feel particularly tumultuous or scary, I sometimes find it reassuring to remember how many times the American people have been tested before. I think about how this country got its start 250 years ago. I think about the courage and power of Civil Rights leaders who created generational change, about the millions of young people who organized against an unjust war in Vietnam. It’s not fair — but fighting for justice and democracy is interwoven in every part of our history, and we are held up by all those who came before us. There is still hope and a path forward.
So we can’t let up — for Alex Pretti and Renee Good, for Liam Ramos and every child who’s been kidnapped, for every person who’s been wrongly detained and deported, for every kid growing up in this moment in America.
Here are ways to act:
→ To support folks on the ground in Minnesota, consider donating to mutual aid groups, crowdfunding campaigns, and organizations helping with legal support: StandWithMinnesota.com [http://standwithminnesota.com] .
→ If you can’t afford to donate, your time and voice are so valuable — please amplify what’s actually happening to drown out the disinformation circulating online.
→ The other really important thing right now is to make sure the Senate doesn’t give DHS another penny. They’re likely to vote on a funding bill this week that would increase the budget given to DHS, including ICE and CBP, but they can’t pass it without Democratic votes. (I proudly voted NO on this same bill in the House last week.) Call the Capitol Switchboard at (202) 224-3141 and urge your Senators to block DHS funding.
Finally, I’ve said this before, but it’s too important not to repeat over and over: the most critical thing we can do to stop a backslide into authoritarianism is to stay in community with one another. Division, isolation, disillusionment, exhaustion — these are tactics , and giving in to them is the worst thing we can do right now.
Connect with your friends, your family, your neighbors, your colleagues. Even if you don’t agree on everything. If you just agree that democracy must be protected, that can be enough to make a difference.
Thank you for reading, and thank you for being a part of my community. I’m grateful to have you here, and I will keep fighting for you.
Sincerely,
Sara

Congresswoman Sara Jacobs
California’s 51st District

clean_body: [] It’s the most gut-wrenching, unspeakable deja vu. Another person executed by federal immigration agents in Minneapolis. Another endless stream of grainy videos, analyzing every angle of a human being’s last moments. Another surge of lies, gaslighting, and blatant disinformation from the Trump administration as they cover up and justify a brutal murder. It’s all so dystopian. I’m incredibly sad for every person who knew and loved Alex Pretti. And I’m angry for them, too. I’m angry for all of us. This is not OK. This is not what democratic governments do. We can’t accept it, and we don’t have to. It’s not fair that we’re facing this reality. We shouldn’t have to be. But when things feel particularly tumultuous or scary, I sometimes find it reassuring to remember how many times the American people have been tested before. I think about how this country got its start 250 years ago. I think about the courage and power of Civil Rights leaders who created generational change, about the millions of young people who organized against an unjust war in Vietnam. It’s not fair — but fighting for justice and democracy is interwoven in every part of our history, and we are held up by all those who came before us. There is still hope and a path forward. So we can’t let up — for Alex Pretti and Renee Good, for Liam Ramos and every child who’s been kidnapped, for every person who’s been wrongly detained and deported, for every kid growing up in this moment in America. Here are ways to act: → To support folks on the ground in Minnesota, consider donating to mutual aid groups, crowdfunding campaigns, and organizations helping with legal support: StandWithMinnesota.com [] . → If you can’t afford to donate, your time and voice are so valuable — please amplify what’s actually happening to drown out the disinformation circulating online. → The other really important thing right now is to make sure the Senate doesn’t give DHS another penny. They’re likely to vote on a funding bill this week that would increase the budget given to DHS, including ICE and CBP, but they can’t pass it without Democratic votes. (I proudly voted NO on this same bill in the House last week.) Call the Capitol Switchboard at (202) 224-3141 and urge your Senators to block DHS funding. Finally, I’ve said this before, but it’s too important not to repeat over and over: the most critical thing we can do to stop a backslide into authoritarianism is to stay in community with one another. Division, isolation, disillusionment, exhaustion — these are tactics , and giving in to them is the worst thing we can do right now. Connect with your friends, your family, your neighbors, your colleagues. Even if you don’t agree on everything. If you just agree that democracy must be protected, that can be enough to make a difference. Thank you for reading, and thank you for being a part of my community. I’m grateful to have you here, and I will keep fighting for you. Sincerely, Sara [] Congresswoman Sara Jacobs California’s 51st District CONTRIBUTE [] [] Paid for by Sara Jacobs for Congress Sara Jacobs for Congress PO Box 120085 San Diego, CA 92112 United States www.facebook.com/SaraJacobsCA/ [www.facebook.com/SaraJacobsCA/] twitter.com/sarajacobsca [twitter.com/sarajacobsca] www.instagram.com/sarajacobsca/ [www.instagram.com/sarajacobsca/] CONTRIBUTE TODAY [] We believe these emails are a critical way for Sara to stay in touch with supporters and constituents. name: Senator Jennifer B. Boysko
subject: Boysko Bulletin: Update on Storm Fern
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/102624/images/Bulletin.jpeg]
Boysko Bulletin: Update on Winter Storm Fern
Dear Peter,
The snow has turned to freezing rain and sleet and cold temperatures will last throughout the week. The National Weather Service has updated the storm to a winter storm warning [https://forecast.weather.gov/showsigwx.php?warnzone=VAZ053&warncounty=VAC059&firewxzone=VAZ053&local_place1=Herndon%20VA&product1=Winter+Storm+Warning&lat=38.9776&lon=-77.3841] , expected to last through January 26 at 4 am, with lasting effects on roads and possibly impacts on trees and electricity due to ice. Please stay safe and heed the warning: “Travel could be nearly impossible overnight, and the Monday morning commute will be very treacherous due to ongoing cold conditions and lingering sleet and snow packed roadways.”
Fairfax County and VDOT are encouraging residents to stay off the roads if possible, so that crews can continue plowing and treating the roads, and also for your safety as some roads remain very slick. VDOT crews also need the ability to respond to an emergency if necessary, clearing the way for emergency personnel to reach an incident."
Fairfax County government offices and schools are closed tomorrow. Federal Government Offices are also closed [https://www.opm.gov/policy-data-oversight/snow-dismissal-procedures/current-status/] with remote and telework employees expected to work.
You can help keep the roads safe by parking in driveways or on a single side of the street to allow a wider path for plows. Learn more about snow removal by visiting virginiadot.org/snow [https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001nBboXVPW_2enZHBbkfjSjZC-6DAWHbKeOZ7Tkavb1jUPxd4UrdVJzT9rxbVbauuJLTkT-cwh4OYMh37vU881QITtjjAC_KB6oS0s9IX6CmdhI_7hbmD3PkzUn_8EvQWll3wAgOKbFHzcTMSqd0PZgR64hNyyMuNcpZsBTcCeTyo=&c=5RJM-HGqzp475DPqriqgP1LcKGgFEJqSCUfa4SBEEsmgQeih_vg4vQ==&ch=gekid5Z1hMFtOPjdDjP6IxQaPPi0m6Wm5BReGovxLjSHWwX6SzIEVA==] .
I’ve included storm resources below. Please be careful and stay safe. Enjoy the beauty of the snow.
Kindest regards,
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/102624/images/2024%20February%20Images/electronic%20signature.png]
Be Prepared - Storm Resources
National Weather Service website [https://www.weather.gov/]
VDOT Road Conditions: https://bit.ly/38wa8tX [https://bit.ly/38wa8tX] or the 511 mobile app for traffic conditions and lane closures.
VDOT Report a Road Problem: https://my.vdot.virginia.gov/ [https://my.vdot.virginia.gov/]
Police, Fire, Ambulance Emergency: call or text 911
Public Safety Non-Emergency: (703) 691-2131 | TTY (703) 204-2264
Report and Check on Power Outages: Dominion Energy [https://www.dominionenergy.com/virginia/report-outage-or-emergency] or call 1 (866)-366-4357 | TTY 711
Washington Gas: 1 (800)-752-7520 or (703) 750-7944 | TTY 711
NOVEC (Northern Virginia Electric Cooperative): 1 (888)-335-0500 or (703) 335-0500 | TTY 711
Fairfax Water: 703-698-5800, after hours: 703-698-5613
Keep in Touch
I’m excited to be back in Richmond for the 2026 General Assembly Session. During Session, I work with my Chief of Staff Karen Harrison, session aides Toby Anthony and Mallory Lenz, interns from Hollins University - Mary Sheetz and Via Adongo, and interns from the University of Richmond, Morgan Koop and Michelle Bergman, and in District, Kathy Shupe, Juliet Morro, and Aditi Goel. For constituent services and to schedule meetings with your community group, please call (703) 437-0086 or email SenatorBoysko@senate.virginia.gov. Friend me on Facebook [https://bit.ly/2PskgOp] and follow me on X [https://bit.ly/3uLhVhC] and Instagram [https://www.instagram.com/jenniferboysko/?hl=en] .
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe.
Friends of Jennifer Boysko
PO Box 247
Herndon, VA 20172
United States

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: Sunday News Digest: 1/25/2026
body:
The ICE Fight Is Already Here in Colorado
| | | | [](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
---
**January 25, 2026** **SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST**
| | Dear Rocky Mountain Voice family,
This week, few issues cut more clearly through the noise in Colorado than immigration enforcement — specifically, how state leaders are choosing to confront, restrict, and reframe the role of ICE within our borders.
While national headlines swirl, the reality is that Colorado doesn’t need outside examples to understand what’s happening. The fight over ICE is already playing out here — in our legislature, our courts, our communities, and increasingly, in our streets.
**In small-town Hudson** , [protests erupted outside an ICE detention facility](https://link.email.joinrmv.), with activists framing the center as an abuse of power while ignoring the basic facts of who ICE is holding and why. Our reporting dug into that disconnect, highlighting the [growing hypocrisy](https://link.email.joinrmv.) in how enforcement is portrayed versus how public safety is actually affected. It’s a pattern we’ve seen repeatedly: emotion first, facts later — if at all.
**Inside the Capitol** , that same posture is now taking legislative form.
Lawmakers opened the session by [advancing new immigration-related bills](https://link.email.joinrmv.) that go well beyond limiting cooperation with federal authorities. One proposal would open the door to lawsuits against any person — not just government officials — over alleged violations connected to immigration enforcement. _[We're calling it the "Sue Anyone Bill."](https://link.email.joinrmv.)_ Sheriffs across the state warn this kind of language doesn’t protect civil rights; it chills law enforcement, discourages cooperation, and pushes public safety decisions into courtrooms after the fact.
At the same time, Colorado remains [locked in active federal litigation](https://link.email.joinrmv.) over previous laws restricting ICE cooperation — a reality largely absent from the rhetoric driving these new bills. Instead of reassessing policies already under legal challenge, legislative leaders appear determined to double down. [Watch this video for an explanation.](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
That defiance was also on display this week as a federal court dealt a setback to Rep. Jason Crow’s attempt to [loosen oversight rules surrounding ICE facilities](https://link.email.joinrmv.). The ruling didn’t resolve the broader legal questions — but it underscored that federal authority still matters, even when Colorado politicians would prefer to sidestep it.
Layered on top of all this is a coordinated effort — by activists and sympathetic media — to recast enforcement itself as oppression. Our commentary this week unpacked how quickly obstruction, interference, and even criminal conduct are being rewritten as victimhood, while [key facts are buried or ignored](https://link.email.joinrmv.).
**Colorado isn’t just limiting cooperation with ICE — it’s building a legal, political, and cultural framework designed to discourage enforcement altogether, regardless of consequences.**
That’s why our editorial board is continuing to call attention to Attorney General Phil Weiser’s so-called [“report ICE” portal](https://link.email.joinrmv.) — and urging Coloradans to _flip the script by reporting actual criminal activity instead_. If state leaders insist on monitoring enforcement, they should also be held accountable for ignoring the real public safety concerns playing out across the state. More on this coming this week.
This debate isn’t abstract. It’s happening now, in Colorado, with real legal and real-world consequences. And as the session unfolds, we’ll continue tracking where policy ends — and ideology begins.
As always, we encourage you to read the full reporting linked throughout, share it, and stay engaged. This is one of those moments where paying attention REALLY matters. While it’s tempting to watch the national narrative here, we urge you to STAY LOCAL. **Because the fight is happening HERE in Colorado, and it’s very real.**
— Team RMV
---
| | |
---
| | **TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK**
---
**[Beyond the baby bust: Parents quietly exit Colorado public schools](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY CHRISTIAN HORSTMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.) _Colorado’s public schools just posted their steepest enrollment slide since the pandemic: down more than 10,000 students this year, affecting each of our top ten largest districts and many others statewide._

**[SB26-005: Colorado Bill Opens State Suits for ICE-Related Rights Violations—Even Against Private Actors](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY SHAINA COLE, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.) Colorado lawmakers have introduced Senate Bill 26-005—legislation that would allow lawsuits in state court when an individual claims their rights under federal law have been violated as a result of civil immigration law enforcement.
**[Homelessness Isn’t Just About Rent: Denver’s Spending Tells a Bigger Story](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY SHAINA COLE, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.) Denver once bought a motel to help move people off the street. Two years later, it made headlines for trying to sell that same property for ten dollars. Denver’s homelessness debate almost always circles back to housing. But recent data suggest the drivers run deeper than housing alone.
|
---
**LEGISLATIVE SESSION**
**[A new session, same defiance: How Colorado’s immigration policy put ideology over enforcement](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.) Colorado lawmakers are walking into the 2026 session with the budget already tight. They’re also bracing for more legal fights with Washington. New bills tied to ICE enforcement are moving early, including one that would expand the state’s ability to sue over immigration-related rights claims.

**[‘Fully funded’ education: Panelist cites 30–70% increase as Sen. Kipp says “TABOR is why we don’t have nice things”](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY COLLEEN ENOS, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.) _The Joint Education Committee SMART Act (State Measurement for Accountable, Responsive, and Transparent Government) hearing was held after the session adjourned for the day. The presentation brought out the points that education is the second largest budget item for Colorado, public school enrollment is decreasing, the birth rate is declining, and Colorado public education needs much, much more money.__
_
**[Runaway Medicaid Spending Forces Colorado Toward Hard Choices](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** [COMPLETE COLORADO](https://link.email.joinrmv.) The legislature’s Joint Budget Committee (JBC) recently held a hearing with the Department of Healthcare Policy and Financing (HCPF), the Governor’s office, and Manatt, a healthcare consulting firm, to address the unsustainable growth of Colorado’s Medicaid spending.
**[READ MORE ON LEGISLATIVE SESSION](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**

|
---
**STATE**
**[How Colorado’s caucus system works—and why the 2026 midterm elections start now](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.) If you’ve ever heard the word “caucus” and quietly thought, I’m not entirely sure what that actually means, you’re not alone. Even Eric Trump has admitted he had to ask. While campaigning for his father during the 2016 election, he recalls turning to organizers and asking, “Can someone finally explain what this caucus is?”
**[State Prison Investigates Incident Involving Tina Peters at La Vista Facility](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)[DENVER7](https://link.email.joinrmv.) Tina Peters, a former Mesa County clerk who was sentenced to nine years behind bars for her role in an election security breach in 2021, was involved in an incident with another inmate at La Vista Correctional Facility over the weekend.
**[Federal judge recommends dismissing school districts’ lawsuit over transgender sports rules](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**[COLORADO POLITICS](https://link.email.joinrmv.) A judge has recommended dismissing a federal lawsuit filed by multiple school districts against the Colorado Attorney General’s and Civil Rights Offices over their policies dictating students’ participation in athletics and activities based on their biological sex rather than gender identity.

_**[READ MORE STATE NEWS](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**_
|
---
**NATIONAL**
**[Why Congress keeps pressing NIH over bat research funding tied to CSU](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.) Congress is demanding more transparency from the NIH over bat research grants tied to Colorado State University, asking, “How many millions of tax dollars is NIH giving to live bat research and why?
**[SAVE Act Gains Momentum as GOP Pushes Citizenship Verification to Vote](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**[JUST THE NEWS](https://link.email.joinrmv.) Two Senate Republicans on Wednesday rallied behind the House-passed Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) Act, which would require documentary proof of U.S. citizenship when registering to vote for federal elections.
**[Court Deals Setback to Rep Jason Crow Over ICE Oversight Rules Challenge](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**[COLORADO POLITICS](https://link.email.joinrmv.) A federal judge refused Monday to temporarily block the Trump administration from enforcing a new policy requiring a week’s notice before members of Congress can visit immigration detention facilities.

_**[READ MORE NATIONAL NEWS](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**_
|
---
**LOCAL**
**[Colorado Medical Device Executives Indicted in Massive $873M Fraud Case](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**[DENVER7](https://link.email.joinrmv.) A federal grand jury has charged two former executives of a Colorado medical device company with planning to cheat health care programs and investors, and with other types of fraud.

**[Federal Lawsuit Challenges Northglenn Over Church Activities At Public Park](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**[COMPLETE COLORADO](https://link.email.joinrmv.) Several recent legal motions related to an ongoing lawsuit against the City of Northglenn seek dismissal of citations issued to members of three Colorado churches for hosting a public worship and charity event at a city park.

**[State Regulators Set Hearing on El Paso County Rejection of Xcel Power Line Permits](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**[DENVER GAZETTE](https://link.email.joinrmv.) Colorado regulators plan a public hearing next week on Xcel Energy’s appeal to override El Paso County’s rejection of permits for a major transmission line segment. The hearing renews debate over a statewide renewable energy project that rural residents argue threatens property rights, wildfire safety and local landscapes without delivering benefits to their communities.
**_[READ MORE LOCAL NEWS](https://link.email.joinrmv.)_**
|
---
**COMMENTARY**
**[Colorado’s prisons are on the verge of total collapse.](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY AHNAF KALAM, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.) _Colorado’s prisons are on the verge of total collapse. As of late 2025, state facilities are operating at nearly 98% capacity, while being severely understaffed. Men’s prisons alone are projected to run out of beds entirely in the next fiscal year unless drastic measures are taken to reduce the population or add capacity._
__
**[Merit Academy: Undeniable Change, A Model For Our Next Victory](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY ERIC GIL, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.) _The success of Merit Academy in Woodland Park is no secret. When your school is ranked #5 of all K-12 Colorado Public Schools, it is hard to hide. This article is a look into the origin story of Merit Academy, led by Headmaster Gwynne Pekron._
__

**[Secure the High Ground: Truth Is Your Terrain](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY DRAKE HUNTER,[ ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.) _Truth is not optional equipment. It’s foundational positioning. Before Scripture tells us what to do, it tells us where to stand. During the Gulf War, I was stationed at Büchel Air Base in Germany._ __

The ruling didn’t resolve the broader legal questions — but it underscored that federal authority still matters, even when Colorado politicians would prefer to sidestep it. Layered on top of all this is a coordinated effort — by activists and sympathetic media — to recast enforcement itself as oppression. Our commentary this week unpacked how quickly obstruction, interference, and even criminal conduct are being rewritten as victimhood, while . Colorado isn’t just limiting cooperation with ICE — it’s building a legal, political, and cultural framework designed to discourage enforcement altogether, regardless of consequences. That’s why our editorial board is continuing to call attention to Attorney General Phil Weiser’s so-called — and urging Coloradans to flip the script by reporting actual criminal activity instead. If state leaders insist on monitoring enforcement, they should also be held accountable for ignoring the real public safety concerns playing out across the state. More on this coming this week. This debate isn’t abstract. It’s happening now, in Colorado, with real legal and real-world consequences. And as the session unfolds, we’ll continue tracking where policy ends — and ideology begins. As always, we encourage you to read the full reporting linked throughout, share it, and stay engaged. This is one of those moments where paying attention REALLY matters. While it’s tempting to watch the national narrative here, we urge you to STAY LOCAL. Because the fight is happening HERE in Colorado, and it’s very real. — Team RMV | | | | | TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK BY CHRISTIAN HORSTMANN, Colorado’s public schools just posted their steepest enrollment slide since the pandemic: down more than 10,000 students this year, affecting each of our top ten largest districts and many others statewide. BY SHAINA COLE, Colorado lawmakers have introduced Senate Bill 26-005—legislation that would allow lawsuits in state court when an individual claims their rights under federal law have been violated as a result of civil immigration law enforcement. BY SHAINA COLE, Denver once bought a motel to help move people off the street. Two years later, it made headlines for trying to sell that same property for ten dollars. Denver’s homelessness debate almost always circles back to housing. But recent data suggest the drivers run deeper than housing alone. | LEGISLATIVE SESSION BY JEN SCHUMANN, Colorado lawmakers are walking into the 2026 session with the budget already tight. They’re also bracing for more legal fights with Washington. New bills tied to ICE enforcement are moving early, including one that would expand the state’s ability to sue over immigration-related rights claims. BY COLLEEN ENOS, The Joint Education Committee SMART Act (State Measurement for Accountable, Responsive, and Transparent Government) hearing was held after the session adjourned for the day. The presentation brought out the points that education is the second largest budget item for Colorado, public school enrollment is decreasing, the birth rate is declining, and Colorado public education needs much, much more money. The legislature’s Joint Budget Committee (JBC) recently held a hearing with the Department of Healthcare Policy and Financing (HCPF), the Governor’s office, and Manatt, a healthcare consulting firm, to address the unsustainable growth of Colorado’s Medicaid spending. | STATE BY JEN SCHUMANN, If you’ve ever heard the word “caucus” and quietly thought, I’m not entirely sure what that actually means, you’re not alone. Even Eric Trump has admitted he had to ask. While campaigning for his father during the 2016 election, he recalls turning to organizers and asking, “Can someone finally explain what this caucus is?” Tina Peters, a former Mesa County clerk who was sentenced to nine years behind bars for her role in an election security breach in 2021, was involved in an incident with another inmate at La Vista Correctional Facility over the weekend. A judge has recommended dismissing a federal lawsuit filed by multiple school districts against the Colorado Attorney General’s and Civil Rights Offices over their policies dictating students’ participation in athletics and activities based on their biological sex rather than gender identity. | NATIONAL BY JEN SCHUMANN, Congress is demanding more transparency from the NIH over bat research grants tied to Colorado State University, asking, “How many millions of tax dollars is NIH giving to live bat research and why? Two Senate Republicans on Wednesday rallied behind the House-passed Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) Act, which would require documentary proof of U.S. citizenship when registering to vote for federal elections. A federal judge refused Monday to temporarily block the Trump administration from enforcing a new policy requiring a week’s notice before members of Congress can visit immigration detention facilities. | LOCAL A federal grand jury has charged two former executives of a Colorado medical device company with planning to cheat health care programs and investors, and with other types of fraud. Several recent legal motions related to an ongoing lawsuit against the City of Northglenn seek dismissal of citations issued to members of three Colorado churches for hosting a public worship and charity event at a city park. Colorado regulators plan a public hearing next week on Xcel Energy’s appeal to override El Paso County’s rejection of permits for a major transmission line segment. The hearing renews debate over a statewide renewable energy project that rural residents argue threatens property rights, wildfire safety and local landscapes without delivering benefits to their communities. | COMMENTARY BY AHNAF KALAM, Colorado’s prisons are on the verge of total collapse. As of late 2025, state facilities are operating at nearly 98% capacity, while being severely understaffed. Men’s prisons alone are projected to run out of beds entirely in the next fiscal year unless drastic measures are taken to reduce the population or add capacity. BY ERIC GIL, The success of Merit Academy in Woodland Park is no secret. When your school is ranked #5 of all K-12 Colorado Public Schools, it is hard to hide. This article is a look into the origin story of Merit Academy, led by Headmaster Gwynne Pekron. BY DRAKE HUNTER, Truth is not optional equipment. It’s foundational positioning. Before Scripture tells us what to do, it tells us where to stand. During the Gulf War, I was stationed at Büchel Air Base in Germany. | Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share? Email mailto:submit@rockymountainvoice.com. | 🥇DID YOU KNOW? The United States holds the record for the most Olympic medals of any country , having surpassed 3,000 total medals across both Summer and Winter Games as of 2024. | The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support! | QUOTE OF THE DAY "You will never reach your destination if you stop and throw stones at every dog that barks." – Winston Churchill | | | | ---|---|--- Did a friend forward you this email? | A product of Rocky Mountain Voice, Inc. 8200 S Quebec St, STE A3601 Centennial, CO 80112 This message was originally sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com Be sure to put “RockyMountainVoice.com” on your safe-senders whitelist. If you ever stop receiving our emails, it might be because someone to whom you forwarded the email unintentionally removed you from the list. subject: Retirement Rules Are Changing - Are Yours Still Protected?
body:
---

---

---
_**A Special Message from our Sponsor**_ _Please note that views expressed in this message do not necessarily reflect those of_ _Parler_ _and do not constitute an endorsement or recommendation by_ _Parler_ _._
---
| Allegiance Gold | [](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D) | **[You Assume Your Bank Account is Yours - Until It Isn't.
](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)**Your bank doesn't need your permission to freeze or seize funds during **"financial stress."** When that happens, access disappears first and explanations come later. [**Learn More**](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)
---|---
[](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D) | **[When Power Fights Power, Your Money Is the One That Takes the Hit.
](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)**As Trump challenges the Federal Reserve and major financial institutions, the fallout doesn't stay in Washington. It shows up in tighter credit, weaker dollars, and shrinking financial freedom. Waiting doesn't protect you - **it quietly puts you at risk.** [
**See Your Options**](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)
[](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D) | **[The Fear Isn't Losing Money - It's Losing the Life You Planned For.
](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)**Rising costs, unstable markets, geopolitical chaos and a continuing weakening dollar are **making Americans wonder** if their savings could truly support retirement. [
**Find Out More**](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)
* * *
**[Retirement Rules are Shifting](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)** **and no one sends a notice when it happens.** You don’t wake up to an alert. You don’t get a warning. Everything looks normal—until it isn’t. Statements still arrive. Balances still show. But the rules underneath them start to change. **[Quietly.](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)** That’s what unsettles people right now. Not a crash. Not a headline. The feeling that control isn’t what it used to be. You don’t lose retirement security all at once. **[You lose it when:](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)**
* Options narrow
* Access becomes conditional
* Decisions start depending on rules you don't set

By the time it’s obvious, reacting calmly is no longer possible. That’s when regret shows up. When systems feel less predictable, people start asking different questions. Not _“What’s my return?”_ But _**["What do I actually control?"](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)**_ That’s why certain assets like gold—and certain strategies like a gold IRA—always resurface during periods like this. Not because of hype. Because they sit outside the noise. Outside permission. And outside sudden rule changes. **[Learn how Americans are quietly reassessing their retirement—and how a gold IRA can help restore control while options still exist.](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)** [](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D) **FREE for a Limited Time!**

name: Mark Kelly
subject: Do not be afraid. Do not be silent. Speak out.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by No Dem Left Behind PAC Not Authorized by Any Federal Candidate or Candidate's Committee
body:
| Folks, it's Mark Kelly. We all remember what happened five years ago this month. Donald Trump sent a mob to the Capitol to steal an election.

He had just lost an election, fair and square, and he didn’t like the result. So he decided he was going to try to silence the voices of 81 million people. It was one of the darkest days in the history of our country, but we made it through.

Now he’s back, five years later, and it’s the same scheme. His Secretary of Defense, Pete Hegseth, is trying to silence me as we speak. He thinks he can make an example out of me, and intimidate others into staying quiet.

But here’s the thing: Americans really like freedom of speech. You sign an oath to defend it when you join the military. Trying to silence people into compliance is just about the most un-American thing you can do. Period. There is nothing Trump or Hegseth or anybody in that administration can do to shut me up. I am going to keep fighting for our people, our democracy, and our Constitution. We’re not going to let him win. Not a chance. So, five years on from January 6th, I want you to take this to heart: **Do not be afraid. Do not be silent. Speak out.** We’re going to get through this the same way we did back then: together. **[Please, split a grassroots donation between No Dem Left Behind and me to help support our fight against Trump. This is our toughest yet, and I need a huge team behind me.](https://nodem-lb.com/l/bfuQN2BE7cy0)**
---
[ CHIP IN TODAY! ](https://nodem-lb.com/l/XfESHUTvtiTQ)
---
Thank you,
Mark

He had just lost an election, fair and square, and he didn’t like the result. So he decided he was going to try to silence the voices of 81 million people. It was one of the darkest days in the history of our country, but we made it through. Now he’s back, five years later, and it’s the same scheme. His Secretary of Defense, Pete Hegseth, is trying to silence me as we speak. He thinks he can make an example out of me, and intimidate others into staying quiet. But here’s the thing: Americans really like freedom of speech. You sign an oath to defend it when you join the military. Trying to silence people into compliance is just about the most un-American thing you can do. Period. There is nothing Trump or Hegseth or anybody in that administration can do to shut me up. I am going to keep fighting for our people, our democracy, and our Constitution. We’re not going to let him win. Not a chance. So, five years on from January 6th, I want you to take this to heart: Do not be afraid. Do not be silent. Speak out. We’re going to get through this the same way we did back then: together. Thank you, Mark | | | Mail checks to: No Dem Left Behind 1030 15th St NW #404 Washington, DC 20005 | Paid for by No Dem Left Behind PAC Not Authorized by Any Federal Candidate or Candidate's Committee.
