subject: Grid Emergency, Taipei 101 Scaling, and Dog's New Snow Boots
body:
Power outages stayed above 1 million customers on Sunday as [Winter Storm Fern dumped heavy ice](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOozAYhJ8GOkf2j_nBhYtr0uXaK5FjD8Qng5HxBuXtV-wrbDkaaTTf9-8xPVxMcVv-uhW2znnacB4JtaJMS4kBK8qCzX-qizuikurwbiJJLKQSxFNwMX0m3QY7A4SuhVUDG019b2T7sqPx_ciDkmMPw8rNGn6gjhV5HzqMbbTXmlTESkkmc6MAz9A8a_YcWDdaIsSay-1_jlt9YU6f_Ea5-by2yb5q3Y-m-9PQvaG72y-Aq7pCqdEnNHQ_41ZRxFFzWcWx5VNED7HnE0Xkr-oWHGKAU7ozppfE4DE8NSGop3bwnWH_bIsN-xlTigcPjZbL6mL6OVF_Ze5t6TsAAP__gc2AWw) across the South and mid-Atlantic, snapping trees onto distribution lines and slowing restoration in the hardest-hit areas.

| | |
---
| [](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O2zAMgJ_G3hxIlMzYg4YCh2zXtaOgSLTDQj-GLOeQty-cFn2BW_l9IInv16f9dBw5rz9dItOWYjN97ZFao2rXyoES1ZWyfzXHG7EUcvfOggAchBwAbXAcX1b3wSxEQKonI684axjHWfQPgzONSoWJphnCfVRK3XECAqecWmaPPZtzm5CAUgqE-QKBPJLGRaPHgLrTggK3Ui-_C-f2oCW-ypPqxZfUR_Nobds79aODWwc3zk_a2yVxKJWHO5fTOpG6HS3ZvRzVU6c-KDmOHeA5TBT4SJ362ByHYXO1Zar7g7d_3Lu0OV7z2_gP5SQGNSr7N8ORuTkrUQHqab5K7KsJ2xfHyDteOy3W8-D75fatzk8DfwIAAP__dr6QSQ) | |
# Monday, January 26, 2026
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1926, Scottish inventor John Logie Baird demonstrated the [first true television](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFugzAQxvGnMZuRfZhLGDx0YUvXjsjBB1xkILKvQbx9lapv0PknfZ_0_7oNt8CJt_kzrORl2oeNjpJIhPIwZ460Up5pG08J_CS2xpYxDGAAtbEacIiB0zm4KvqJCKipyNsLdg7atjPV4vHaXMZ7d-1CcNDh5JzFCG1Dk2kaaGPF_r1mLKC1BqGrIdKI5HByOGJEp5yhyLLn-rHzJgtN6dxflOtxX6vkF5FnUc2Hgl5BfxxHvXCRPZ9vV9DLwkXHcGre9J8o6B9h-w751IAK-nvgHHWkdd-K5CBUtLyq7OPz4JS44EU5M6-B0--n_CvUy8NPAAAA__--B3xd) set in London. Baird operated two ventriloquist dummies in front of a camera out of the audience's view, transmitting the moving image onto a screen. Humans have an odd habit of doing wildly unnecessary [things at great heights](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OpDAQhJ8GMo_aDTQQOLhksrn0QmTsNvTJYGSs-Xn7FbuPsFmVSirVV_8e08NKlH35azc2JaRp59cZuRTO05LF88Z54d19ipWDRYM-nZ0QkBRohTR5K_EztbU3gRm5qdnonsYWu26EejXzjH3fhCFgj51zAWY_d4iaeLDkkWoxVxtoJK2BcLyhZ0fcUmjJkae2aoG9lJRv_5PsZeUQP-nJ-ebSVkezlnKcVfOnwnuFd3tcAFd0mVzERb5U5Lda076n6NUPitKg1ZliUi7KNqudS4jyVi7wMBJg0-MIMJDzzN2goe-gozCHOht_vCRGOamvWlg2K_F7S_nVgU-DXwEAAP__BWmB_A), and on Sunday, Alex Honnold's scaling Taipei 101 without ropes was one of them. While the rest of us felt our palms sweat watching him climb, it gave us all a moment to marvel at human daring, as we cover below. With just 15% of shares remaining, _The Flyover’s_ current fundraising round is nearing its end, and the window to participate is closing fast. Most people miss this moment, so don’t be one of them and [invest now](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD_v2jAQQD-Ns4H8LyYMHirRbJQFqaNl7Eu4yn8i5wDx7au0dOv2W9-7ezrdz7M7e0xY5h8-g6WpugKvNQERNDc3jJChzVDCmzwugIKLNXgnuTQ7LnbSuOgxvZ3uop0AJKgOrDiYo5Z9f-Td3Q781t98GIIa-l4Ppg_DxKeb5GFQh15Dh3arcSGNENzI415GCAa0mbQJJhrNNIeIVNv-V8VCd5jSuz6h7UPNXbJ3omVl6huTI5Mjlies9J_JTavxQdmt9dECMHX6HpGwFibNhjNEfGSmTpdXgbg7Q0T_UcHnxeNcmDqNf4v_RC0Ehbal6_hhBG2rXMeLE9JI0zUblxemhKs5MM3n7DH9uZ2-9PCnlb8DAAD__1cdlKE). More details below. Today’s sponsor is [Midori Bio](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O2zAMgJ_G3hxIlMzYg4YCh2zXtaOgSLTDQj-GLOeQty-cFn2BW_l9IInv16f9dBw5rz9dItOWYjN97ZFao2rXyoES1ZWyfzXHG7EUcvfOggAchBwAbXAcX1b3wSxEQKonI684axjHWfQPgzONSoWJphnCfVRK3XECAqecWmaPPZtzm5CAUgqE-QKBPJLGRaPHgLrTggK3Ui-_C-f2oCW-ypPqxZfUR_Nobds79aODWwc3zk_a2yVxKJWHO5fTOpG6HS3ZvRzVU6c-KDmOHeA5TBT4SJ362ByHYXO1Zar7g7d_3Lu0OV7z2_gP5SQGNSr7N8ORuTkrUQHqab5K7KsJ2xfHyDteOy3W8-D75fatzk8DfwIAAP__dr6QSQ), the company helping plastic producers meet tougher environmental mandates with a simple additive upgrade—no factory downtime or major capital costs.
|
---
## Winter Storm Fern Triggers Grid Emergency
Power outages stayed above 1 million customers on Sunday as [Winter Storm Fern dumped heavy ice](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOozAYhJ8GOkf2j_nBhYtr0uXaK5FjD8Qng5HxBuXtV-wrbDkaaTTf9-8xPVxMcVv-uhW2znnacB4JtaJMS4kBK8qCzX-qizuikurwbiJJLKQSxFNwMX0m3QY7A4SuhVUDG019b2T7sqPx_ciDkmMPw8rNGn6gjhV5HzqMbbTXmlTESkkmc6MAz9A8a_YcWDdaIsSay-1_jlt9YU6f_Ea5-by2yb5q3Y-m-9PQvaG72y-Aq7pCqdEnNHQ_41ZRxFFzWcWx5VNED7HnE0Xkr-oWHGKAU7ozppfE4DE8NSGop3bwnWH_bIsN-xlTigcPjZbL6mL6OVF_Ze5t6TsAAP__gc2AWw) across the South and mid-Atlantic, snapping trees onto distribution lines and slowing restoration in the hardest-hit areas. [Tennessee accounted](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DGO4yAUBuDT4C4WPAixC4pt3GXbLS0Mvx1W2Fjwkii3H81cYS7wFd-_-3z3Kadj--t3OF7LfODdMphR562miB11wxE-7NOJpKRqwc8kyV6kupCdo0_5M5suuhUg6A5O3exo6HodZfdwahm8j8sNwarBjFqvy6IHeAp6CIvVXXLfmlRklZKWxp4igoWxq7HBRmuEkYiJS-3_l3TwA2v-lBdqH8reZfdgPpvQfwRNgqazvFHLk_2G_tkETb7CC5oae4agiXEcaA3oqovnO-Wcmr0JI7fdp_xD8q8eXo6-AgAA__89iHFT) for roughly 300,000 outages on Sunday, with Mississippi and Louisiana each around 150,000, and Georgia and Texas each around 100,000, as utilities warned repairs could take days in the hardest-hit areas. The Department of Energy issued an emergency order on Sunday giving the PJM Interconnection, the largest U.S. regional grid, permission to [run power generation at maximum](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkbGu2zAMRb9G3hRIsiPHg4cUhbfXoUURdDL0JNpmIUsCxcTI3xfu0g_oekgcgvc-PuYPhxHT-s3tMPKS5wRHjcAMNK-EAXagFZJ_s8MCqJWu3s1GGSuVlsbOwWF8z10TxgXAQNvAqHs7dOZ6HVSzjaZvB297rW7e9le9tGYIw02Fm_Wfrv_sGxxPm9LGaq2sGS4mgLfQ2aWz3gbbiU5BQM50-Z0x8QZLfOcX0MXnvYnjxlyqaO_CTMJMx3Fc9prOmTATJPmsJwXHG5AwE-dSORPCiUs-gGSJLrHMT3YrVFmftILEJMFVBjoF0u0YJEFlQs8Q5OrOvVIiQpUuBbkQrhjkvzOO5P2ufz6-_3qIdsoeg2i_fjlzhqP-ACoNjaEcGCNW24tOrbvD-Pcj_q8aXqP5EwAA__8aXJ4s) output and exempting it from certain pollution limits during the storm. The storm underscored how much [the grid depends on natural gas](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHSnCAURp9GOxxAZH8KijR2mzalc5WrexMEh8tqfPvM5hX-_ptvzjm_ntMTKFLafsKOvq55SnhxxFqxTFuhgDuWDdNyV6ADSUnFC0xaaiukEtpOASjek2mDXxE19i169bDO6GFwsn156b6UNqDsPKCUDoYHONfb3tkAcv5SLfnPm1TaKiWtdp0OuFg0djV2scGaxkgMVHPpfmdK9YVrvPOJpVvy3kb_qvXgpv_R6LHR43Vd3Zr_zm-mhMyfTaNHTFi2u9HjDsx0orgoVSyCay674FqAEos3iyNfWMRHXOQDC9RcWPBSYJ8jCjgzBTFHWP7kd-W2-HBcFCOxfTRGbjtQ_E9Vv5Xy9PpfAAAA__8k6Iqq), about 40% of U.S. generation, and how quickly tight pipeline deliveries can become a constraint during extreme weather.
## Democrats Threaten Shutdown Over MN Shooting
Senate leader Chuck Schumer said Democrats won’t provide the votes needed to advance a must-pass spending package if it includes funding for the Department of Homeland Security, raising the [odds of a partial government shutdown](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkE2O3CAQhU-Dd7SgTOP2gkVHkXeTTTTK0kJQtkn4saBmrM7pI3KF2ZVUVZ_e-369rW82xJD3Hzahoa2sGa8WkQjrutfgMWHdMbsX2XBikEI2Z1cQoLmQHPTqbYivVQ3ebIiA44BGTnpWcL_PYjiMmj1MD3ffZnTKTV77x8ON46TcffRWPIZgOk1I0FIKDfMNPDqNSm9KO-21YkqgD1Tq7XcJmQ7c4qt8Yr25koZoDqKzsfHJYGGwXNd1Sy33HYMFM_9oDJZeisFylhgouD56TMVVS43TUdESZr53aE6Yibfjg3y5MrcbYeUp5Iy2fzf-J8Tui8FiK38-5fv736dg41Jc8Gz8_q27xKv9xHoO1fjz6vdNT0yJPdkQ_6emL6n-NPAvAAD__9jZlIY) with money set to run out Friday, Jan. 30. The fight follows the [Saturday's fatal shooting](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOnTAQRb_GdLOyB-MXCoo0dJs2JTL2wJuNsZE9uy_k6yPyC-mudKWje8_P9-Xdc-K8__AHTbKVJdOrJRKhuuyVIx1Ud8rhEs8nsdGmBb-gRgfaALolek7XYrs4bURIfUeTebjR4jCMuntOvXG9XXV8DAHH3ofwoLh-8yMZZ4fVho6nm6YNOmO0w_ENIwVH1m3WBRedVVZTZCn17aNwlidt6SpfVN9CObo0PUXOpvrvCmeFc77O0uSuFM43WOGszZ0HhfNng_ufwlnq53FCLnTAmnwTiHQ0ED7g5dMf8DnChw9lha3SBVupwDmwcN7hrEWoCdUGiX8R-ES_4awkwgrnrk7xfHFK3NxDWb0fntO_qfJffr8m_BsAAP__4NOO5g) of Alex Pretti, 37, an intensive care unit nurse, by a U.S. Border Patrol agent in Minneapolis during an enforcement operation. The Department of Homeland Security says Pretti was armed and resisted as agents tried to disarm him, while local officials and witness accounts dispute key parts of that version, and the Justice Department is investigating. [Key Democrat senators](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHO4yAQhJ8Gd45gjZe4oLjGXa690iKwNnvCYBmSKG9_8r3C385IM_q-P4_l4Thx3n67nWxby5LpUxO1RueynRxop3Oj7L_N8UGspKreLSABe6l6wCU4Tt9Fd8GuREBDR1YZnDSM4yS7aAc0KMfniv6up3FSAZ5GeW9QeUMGQ8f2WpMKUCmJMN0gkEfSuGr0GFALLSlwK-ftb-HcIq3pW9503nzZu2Rja0cVwy8Bs4C5RYqc0tUJmGPZ6QISMFfKrpGAeTRymODeh1j79ZUD563fOWdyR0lc-xpLaVdY46uF8skC5u604fhwSlzRCC233fH_j679SNrbwr8AAAD__2JXgAs) including Angus King, Dick Durbin, and Jacky Rosen who voted to end November's 43-day shutdown announced opposition unless DHS is stripped from the package or includes new accountability measures for immigration enforcement.
## American Scales Taiwan’s Tallest Skyscraper
Professional American rock climber Alex Honnold successfully scaled [Taiwan’s tallest building](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OpDAQhJ8GMo_aDTQQOLhksrn0QmTsNvTJYGSs-Xn7FbuPsFmVSirVV_8e08NKlH35azc2JaRp59cZuRTO05LF88Z54d19ipWDRYM-nZ0QkBRohTR5K_EztbU3gRm5qdnonsYWu26EejXzjH3fhCFgj51zAWY_d4iaeLDkkWoxVxtoJK2BcLyhZ0fcUmjJkae2aoG9lJRv_5PsZeUQP-nJ-ebSVkezlnKcVfOnwnuFd3tcAFd0mVzERb5U5Lda076n6NUPitKg1ZliUi7KNqudS4jyVi7wMBJg0-MIMJDzzN2goe-gozCHOht_vCRGOamvWlg2K_F7S_nVgU-DXwEAAP__BWmB_A), Taipei 101, without any ropes on Sunday as viewers tuned in via a live Netflix broadcast. It took Honnold just over 90 minutes to reach the top of the 101-story, 1,667-foot tower, where he exclaimed, “Sick,” waved his arms to the watching crowd, and took a couple of selfies. [See the photos here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGOpDAQBNCvMZlHduNphsDBJWRz6YXIYzfQt8ZGxmKGv1-xv7BxSU9V9e85Ph1HTvNft5KtUx4TvfdItVIZ58KBViozJX9WxxuxVnr3bgQFKJWWgGNwHM_RNMFOREBtQ1Z32Bu433vVLPaFBL3rVLgb_XAOX6prewyKtA7KPKhhe2lKA2qtEPobBPJIBieDHgMaYRQFrrnc_mdOdaEpnvmgcvN5baJdat120f4RMAgYDleY6nllAoZLFjDUQ8BwDRMwuEgfueSUcgxy_zp3X9xGRfrI60smqlPkj4x8kORUqRxMb6mhRTQKdCdgaIoN25tj5B07YdS8Oo4_ZeqvHjwsfAcAAP__btiDCw). Honnold said the [most challenging](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFyhCAURb9GOxx4smwsKNLYbdqUDsLTJUFwHm_X-PeZzS-kvjN3zjmft-nmYop5_XAbWl7KlPGoCZmRppViwA1pxexPdnHHqKSq3k0gwQipBJgpuJjOSbfBLoiAfYtWXc2g4XIZZHu32M_92ywHrxYvF23CrOZe-YsHOXsE1Ub7epMKjFLSwNBBQG9Qm0Ubb4LRjZYYIhfqvkrMfMclneWJ1Pmytcnemffa9O8NjA2Mx3F0S_l5Wbz2BkYm98TUwFgyis1lwQ_KGET9PqsntyOJo1AKVWylsggur0jlUUXAyjE7jiWLnZD5FPVBhC61ZMN-xJRiNddGy3VzMf3h8L8aPi38BgAA__-fwolD) part of the climb was not getting complacent about scaling the 64 decorative dragon formations en route, and he explained his tiredness near the top as the wind whipped against the corner of the building he was climbing. Used to climbing in remote areas, Honnold said the watching crowd was unnerving at first before noting, “ _I mean basically it just makes the whole experience feel almost more festive. All these nice people are out supporting me and having a good time._ ”
* * *

**Most People Miss This** We want to be very straightforward with _The Flyover_ readers. In the final days of an investment round, almost everyone is paying attention—but very few people are still able to act. Just days ago, we announced there are 10 days left to invest in _The Flyover_. Today, that window is even smaller. With roughly 15% of shares remaining, we are now in the final allocation phase of this round. **This is the part most people miss.** Not because they don’t believe in the company.
Not because they don’t support the mission.
But because they assume there will be more time. There won’t be. Once the remaining shares are allocated, this round closes—regardless of the calendar.
No extensions.
No reopening.
No second chance. What makes this moment different isn’t persuasion—it’s availability. **_The Flyover_ is already what most media startups never become:**
* Profitable
* Growing rapidly

clean_body: Power outages stayed above 1 million customers on Sunday as across the South and mid-Atlantic, snapping trees onto distribution lines and slowing restoration in the hardest-hit areas. | | | | | | Monday, January 26, 2026 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1926, Scottish inventor John Logie Baird demonstrated the set in London. Baird operated two ventriloquist dummies in front of a camera out of the audience's view, transmitting the moving image onto a screen. Humans have an odd habit of doing wildly unnecessary , and on Sunday, Alex Honnold's scaling Taipei 101 without ropes was one of them. While the rest of us felt our palms sweat watching him climb, it gave us all a moment to marvel at human daring, as we cover below. With just 15% of shares remaining, The Flyover’s current fundraising round is nearing its end, and the window to participate is closing fast. Most people miss this moment, so don’t be one of them and . More details below. Today’s sponsor is , the company helping plastic producers meet tougher environmental mandates with a simple additive upgrade—no factory downtime or major capital costs. | Winter Storm Fern Triggers Grid Emergency Power outages stayed above 1 million customers on Sunday as across the South and mid-Atlantic, snapping trees onto distribution lines and slowing restoration in the hardest-hit areas. for roughly 300,000 outages on Sunday, with Mississippi and Louisiana each around 150,000, and Georgia and Texas each around 100,000, as utilities warned repairs could take days in the hardest-hit areas. The Department of Energy issued an emergency order on Sunday giving the PJM Interconnection, the largest U.S. regional grid, permission to output and exempting it from certain pollution limits during the storm. The storm underscored how much , about 40% of U.S. generation, and how quickly tight pipeline deliveries can become a constraint during extreme weather. Democrats Threaten Shutdown Over MN Shooting Senate leader Chuck Schumer said Democrats won’t provide the votes needed to advance a must-pass spending package if it includes funding for the Department of Homeland Security, raising the with money set to run out Friday, Jan. 30. The fight follows the of Alex Pretti, 37, an intensive care unit nurse, by a U.S. Border Patrol agent in Minneapolis during an enforcement operation. The Department of Homeland Security says Pretti was armed and resisted as agents tried to disarm him, while local officials and witness accounts dispute key parts of that version, and the Justice Department is investigating. including Angus King, Dick Durbin, and Jacky Rosen who voted to end November's 43-day shutdown announced opposition unless DHS is stripped from the package or includes new accountability measures for immigration enforcement. American Scales Taiwan’s Tallest Skyscraper Professional American rock climber Alex Honnold successfully scaled , Taipei 101, without any ropes on Sunday as viewers tuned in via a live Netflix broadcast. It took Honnold just over 90 minutes to reach the top of the 101-story, 1,667-foot tower, where he exclaimed, “Sick,” waved his arms to the watching crowd, and took a couple of selfies. . Honnold said the part of the climb was not getting complacent about scaling the 64 decorative dragon formations en route, and he explained his tiredness near the top as the wind whipped against the corner of the building he was climbing. Used to climbing in remote areas, Honnold said the watching crowd was unnerving at first before noting, “ I mean basically it just makes the whole experience feel almost more festive. All these nice people are out supporting me and having a good time. ” Love reading The Flyover? mailto:?cc=editor@jointheflyover.com&subject=My%20new%20favorite%20newsletter!&body=Hey!%0D%0A%0D%0AI%E2%80%99ve%20been%20reading%20a%20new%20newsletter%20every%20morning%20called%20The%20Flyover.%20I%20love%20it!%20I%20think%20you%27ll%20love%20it%20too.%0D%0A%0D%0AIt%E2%80%99s%20my%20new%20favorite%20source%20for%20news%20first%20thing%20in%20the%20morning.%20They%20cover%20important%20and%20interesting%20stories%E2%80%94usually%20politics%2C%20sports%2C%20finance%2C%20science%20%26%20tech%2C%20etc.%0D%0A%0D%0AI%20think%20you%20should%20give%20it%20a%20try.%0D%0A%0D%0AJust%20copy%20this%20link%20and%20paste%20it%20into%20your%20browser%3A%20https%3A%2F%2Fjointheflyover.com%2F%3Futm%3D83%0D%0A%0D%0ALet%20me%20know%20if%20you%20like%20it.%20I%20think%20you%20will!%0D%0A to share with your friends and family. | Most People Miss This We want to be very straightforward with The Flyover readers. In the final days of an investment round, almost everyone is paying attention—but very few people are still able to act. Just days ago, we announced there are 10 days left to invest in The Flyover. Today, that window is even smaller. With roughly 15% of shares remaining, we are now in the final allocation phase of this round. This is the part most people miss. Not because they don’t believe in the company. Not because they don’t support the mission. But because they assume there will be more time. There won’t be. Once the remaining shares are allocated, this round closes—regardless of the calendar. No extensions. No reopening. No second chance. What makes this moment different isn’t persuasion—it’s availability. The Flyover is already what most media startups never become: Profitable Growing rapidly Trusted by millions of readers and viewers Built without corporate sponsorship or political donors That part isn’t theoretical. It’s already true. What is still optional—for a very short time—is whether you are an owner as the next phase of growth happens. We are actively expanding: Into more states Into deeper audio and video Into products like texting and The Flyover app Into a larger, more durable media company built for the long term Those plans are being funded right now. The only remaining question is who is still able to participate before this round closes. If you’ve been waiting for a clearer signal—this is it. 👉 With roughly 15% of shares remaining, this is the final allocation window. name: Marsha Blackburn
subject: Never expected to get the cold shoulder from YOU
body:
Peter, is everything OK?

| | | | Peter, why have you been giving us the cold shoulder?
I know I’ve reached out this week about this issue.My team tells me they’ve texted and emailed at least twice too!
Is everything ok? Should we take the hint and assume you aren’t interested in these messages?
That would be truly shocking to me since you’ve always been such a steadfast supporter of mine and President Trump’s mission to save America, so I’m giving you one more chance!
[My ONLY request right now is that you complete the Official Trump Voter Profile before the end of the week.](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn.-0WhR1yh8gZ9mtQ) Really, that’s it! I just need your feedback, nothing more! | [ SUBMIT MY TRUMP VOTER PROFILE ](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn.)

# Monday, January 26, 2026
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1839, the Congress of the Republic of Texas passed a [homestead act](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DGOwyAQheHT2J0tGFgcCopt3GXbLa0JjGMkDBGM4vj2q-QKW_3V-4r3e12uGFPM9x_cyfFrLUumoyViprpQw0qNa_QcS26NS909Zk8LCDCDkAOYJWBM56L74G4iWNmTk5OxGi4XK_vNGf9lhJwCrSpo6zEgoiSlLiuS8Eb10b0xIcFIEBLsaCdlpZ6UNhKUvGGnBYXIpY68EdML25rO8qQ6-rL3yW3Mj9ap7w7mDubjOEZuG5acYqax1HsH82c1BDyH2_lOBzNlru_aqa8uPI6YUmxm6rS47xjTx-b_PfJ08BcAAP__j591tA) guaranteeing every citizen or head of family "fifty acres of land or one town lot, including his or her homestead, and improvements not exceeding five hundred dollars in value." ## Ice Knocks Out Power, Cancels School
Downed power lines caused outages affecting over 80,000 customers yesterday. ## Paxton Okays Barring Schools from Education Program
Attorney General Ken Paxton issued an opinion affirming Acting Comptroller Kelly Hancock's authority to [exclude](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL_OozAQxJ8Guo3whhhcuEjDVd9V96dExt58WDJ25N2E5O1P5BWuG2lGP83M36_5y8UU8_dPt5GV163MmXZOJEJ1JnaVWGr0EktmllI377KnGTvU0ClAPQcX03vu22CXLhjVklWDNj2Oo1HtagNeFDlUqG5em2FcRlrQLN1FX0Y_qKWN9oB1CrXCTqE5meFsVD-ce63wrBbX9B2FKKWeZCWhl-Nbepcn1ZMvW5vsKnLn5nxtcGpw2vf9tHE-vAYnyvDgBqdjVIPTR38QcK_x6YSA_VpKYpBIAaSAUK2lRhaGUoEybZEYvMuwEATKR-xZHn6lynB3LykZ2L0PsKtwvarfv_78mNpqw32PKUXWQ9N335uL6dNX_u_lp8V_AQAA__9Zj5OB) schools "illegally tied to terrorists or foreign adversaries" from the state's education savings account program. Greg Abbott has designated a foreign terrorist organization. ## Lake Effect Snow, Steam Fog, and Thundersleet
As the winter storm blanketed much of the state with ice, it also produced some locally rare weather phenomena, including [lake effect snow](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFu6yAUxvGnwVssOCBsBoYrXXlola5VJ4TxIabCJoKTOHn7Kn2Frt8n_Yb_59mdfcppv3z4DS09YnE7Hi0jEVaHzVdsVFOgVPbWqNQt-D2gAw76xMUJtFt8yk-nusXOfDGiQysGbRSMoxHdagNiHBBl5KOWXqERHHQ0MM5qBg6xS_aFcQFaABdgejNII9QglRYgxeyZ4rgkKrWnFQkfvsX8LHesfShbl-1KdG1M_mMwMZiO4-if5Ua3GV__a_EUVianO5P_L9_vb-tX3qccumqX65FyTk0PTPHL5lP-JelvIe4WfgIAAP__BTFyAA). Arlington residents got flurries from snow produced by moisture from Lake Arlington and south winds up to 30 miles per hour. In Austin, [steam fog](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOtCAUhZ9GOwwgP0pB8Td2s-2W5gJXJYMygbvjzNtv3GYfYLtzcpLvJN_nbb5BTPFYP2BHS68lzweeNSERlhkrFKxUoqeYj1opl93D4XGWXGrGBZN6DhDTe1ZtsI4HI1q0YtBGyXE0ot1sP_pgUDvvFzUsfhSuB20Gh2HxzvxTbbQXjAupheRCms4MvRFq6JUWshcOGsUxRMqlow0JX1CX9M5PLJ3Pe5vsRvSoTf-_kVMjp_M8u_sLjmu8KgJtWH4TcymvjZwKFGSVEHa25JXVLZ-VfT3YBWYJ7siowDPWRk5tseFxxpRi1UOj-LpDTD_n9DdlTyu_AwAA__8N5oJm), or sea smoke, was seen on Lake Travis as the cool, dry Arctic air moved in. name: The Maine Wire
subject: Maine State Offices Closed Monday due to Severe Weather
body:
Here's the latest breaking news at The Maine Wire

** Maine State Offices Closed Monday due to Severe Weather (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=cdd679cf86&e=f093fa355c)
------------------------------------------------------------

| I want to ask you to do something simple.
Please circle March 10 on your calendar -- and if you can, [make a donation today to help us win the special election that will replace Marjorie Taylor Greene here in Northwest Georgia.](https://zzejj9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001oAUhN_hXFkLMinmNtNtxwmo9Sio5J0zAOm3NI3a3u4XsicceHQ_y5YAQANe3o3amRdM9jxOILwmmS2tS5ggmwEJGlBP3QFk1hNU0kwZYsV7a1puN39GMbHYBH-UUDBR3tepDDumbZNgDt4yFLByhyh1gybz1eH0BRXuXuanxbwewopkXNRJjBFrP1tvLFxdS5pYA6tU-6yQtz7Cs0k5URU2Ln1od18kwobOUSoX73-y7io1VH7jTeA==&c=ZrDnB0WL0FXYsvsOmmtvRmwjXbypWeO5g0uyrJ0xWmG1dB682G4-Kw==&ch=XIxaIcY049efxaozUBZW6jmsYHnCQNt-_MkGVJaloEAOqzKZK4SUwA==)

| I'm Shawn Harris, and I'm running to bring steady, serious leadership back to a district that's been defined for too long by chaos and embarrassment. After 40 years in uniform, I believe service should look like showing up, listening, and doing the job with dignity.

Special elections move fast. There's no long runway. No chance to "build momentum later." What we raise in the coming weeks determines whether we can fully fund voter outreach before Election Day -- or whether we're forced to scale back.

Republicans are counting on people forgetting this date. They're counting on low turnout. And they're counting on Democrats assuming someone else will step up.
I'm asking you to be the person who does.
[If you're able, please make a special contribution today to help me finish this race strong. Every dollar helps me reach one more voter before March 10.](https://zzejj9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001oAUhN_hXFkLMinmNtNtxwmo9Sio5J0zAOm3NI3a3u4XsicceHQ_y5YAQANe3o3amRdM9jxOILwmmS2tS5ggmwEJGlBP3QFk1hNU0kwZYsV7a1puN39GMbHYBH-UUDBR3tepDDumbZNgDt4yFLByhyh1gybz1eH0BRXuXuanxbwewopkXNRJjBFrP1tvLFxdS5pYA6tU-6yQtz7Cs0k5URU2Ln1od18kwbPikV0xryZ9k1t-LCMMhKQ==&c=ZrDnB0WL0FXYsvsOmmtvRmwjXbypWeO5g0uyrJ0xWmG1dB682G4-Kw==&ch=XIxaIcY049efxaozUBZW6jmsYHnCQNt-_MkGVJaloEAOqzKZK4SUwA==)

clean_body: | | | | If you believe you've received this email in error, you may unsubscribe below. | | | | I want to ask you to do something simple. Please circle March 10 on your calendar -- and if you can, == | | == | I'm Shawn Harris, and I'm running to bring steady, serious leadership back to a district that's been defined for too long by chaos and embarrassment. After 40 years in uniform, I believe service should look like showing up, listening, and doing the job with dignity. Special elections move fast. There's no long runway. No chance to "build momentum later." What we raise in the coming weeks determines whether we can fully fund voter outreach before Election Day -- or whether we're forced to scale back. Republicans are counting on people forgetting this date. They're counting on low turnout. And they're counting on Democrats assuming someone else will step up. I'm asking you to be the person who does. == | | | | | == | == | == | == Thank you for standing with me, Shawn Harris | | == | SHAWN FOR GEORGIA | P.O. Box 1688 | Cedartown, GA 30125 US | | | | | |

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Colorado Draws the Line
body:
| | | | | [](https://6s79z9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wgJ85Z53QKcMkgvmYUymE8zULMWrlRaUtOKR7l1BcHN6_9Y9gDlUToa6lYbVCQgk1-GX7EkMXUykp7FJ8HRJmveFoA28zffrtup2ukRyG2KHPVihA2qOoe99y4tr2nLPdMJU3grdyzkcbaI55ypAFUkgsYW3nSXSQlJU4z32VF64_Gu_t1j_PDuQSz34z7pzA0MS48jSndkVAtKNkImFdsvf-oUzxxHO&c=Nr6qxdfb2OJW7DRGi1SySCUDnTR0Spo-H0EYLt9SYHXF8ThKV29pqA==&ch=k0IvsHJJlCMeKF2zgtdIOYf3zhUgqUz-YbVIH89bQrlXMxue4_RSFg==)
---
Peter,
MAGA entered 2026 with an alarming redistricting advantage.
With states like TX, MO, NC, SC, FL, and UT redrawing their maps to eliminate Democratic seats in Congress, Democrats are facing down the real possibility of losing control of the House not just in 2026, but a decade to come.
But, the calvary has arrived and Democrats are now putting up a valiant defense:
---
| [](https://6s79z9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wgJ85Z53QKcMkgvmYUymE8zULMWrlRaUtOKR7l1BcHN6_9Y9gDlUToa6lYbVCQgk1-GX7EkMXUykp7FJ8HRJmveFoA28zffrtup2ukRyG2KHPVihA2qOoe99y4tr2nLPdMJU3grdyzkcbaI55ypAFUkgsYW3nSXSQlJU4z32VF64_Gu_t1j_PDuQSz34z7pzA0MS48jSndln8zxmd27UgIvbdfH4prrG&c=Nr6qxdfb2OJW7DRGi1SySCUDnTR0Spo-H0EYLt9SYHXF8ThKV29pqA==&ch=k0IvsHJJlCMeKF2zgtdIOYf3zhUgqUz-YbVIH89bQrlXMxue4_RSFg==)
---
Maryland and Virginia have both made moves in the past few days to counter MAGA's election rigging scheme. It helps cut into MAGA's magic number of 18, the amount of seats they need to all but lock in one party rule in Congress for the next decade.
But, it still isn't enough. Especially if the Supreme Court guts a key portion of the Voting Rights Act that would turbocharge MAGA's election rigging efforts and give the GOP as many as 25 seats. That decision could come any day now.
That's why Colorado has thrown its hat in the ring.
Colorado Draws the Line is spearheading a ballot initiative that would give Democrats 3-4 additional seats in Congress in Colorado. This could be the difference in this existential fight for Democrats.
But MAGA has caught wind of our arrival, and they are gearing up to spend millions to defeat us before the initiative even makes it on the ballot. If Democrats from across the country rally to our cause right now, we'll be able to defeat anything they throw at us.
[That's why we're asking: Will you rush a contribution to our ballot measure committee and help us get new maps on the ballot for Colorado? Our average donation is around $30. Many give more. Some give less. The amount doesn't matter; just donate what you can because it all adds up.](https://6s79z9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wgJ85Z53QKcMkgvmYUymE8zULMWrlRaUtOKR7l1BcHN6_9Y9gDlUToa6lYbVCQgkkK32-F7TXm9u2io8pUGu-IZgZO9SN3z6r8swDH3wvov_n4zIA-VgYmvz4WiTSJAHC4snafvh3AWD0vdH4k67zuIxbT_Fdz4TxVp4aK74QKFOKRgZVFbAHeYzx35sxiZsdwLoobaGeCbFvH0iQDhY2lQGRUfDkRX5g-gxCIlM3_w=&c=Nr6qxdfb2OJW7DRGi1SySCUDnTR0Spo-H0EYLt9SYHXF8ThKV29pqA==&ch=k0IvsHJJlCMeKF2zgtdIOYf3zhUgqUz-YbVIH89bQrlXMxue4_RSFg==)

[](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D) | **[When Power Fights Power, Your Money Is the One That Takes the Hit.
](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)**As Trump challenges the Federal Reserve and major financial institutions, the fallout doesn't stay in Washington. It shows up in tighter credit, weaker dollars, and shrinking financial freedom. Waiting doesn't protect you - **it quietly puts you at risk.** [
**See Your Options**](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)
[](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D) | **[The Fear Isn't Losing Money - It's Losing the Life You Planned For.
](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)**Rising costs, unstable markets, geopolitical chaos and a continuing weakening dollar are **making Americans wonder** if their savings could truly support retirement. [
**Find Out More**](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)
* * *
**[Retirement Rules are Shifting](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)** **and no one sends a notice when it happens.** You don’t wake up to an alert. You don’t get a warning. Everything looks normal—until it isn’t. Statements still arrive. Balances still show. But the rules underneath them start to change. **[Quietly.](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)** That’s what unsettles people right now. Not a crash. Not a headline. The feeling that control isn’t what it used to be. You don’t lose retirement security all at once. **[You lose it when:](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)**
* Options narrow
* Access becomes conditional
* Decisions start depending on rules you don't set

By the time it’s obvious, reacting calmly is no longer possible. That’s when regret shows up. When systems feel less predictable, people start asking different questions. Not _“What’s my return?”_ But _**["What do I actually control?"](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)**_ That’s why certain assets like gold—and certain strategies like a gold IRA—always resurface during periods like this. Not because of hype. Because they sit outside the noise. Outside permission. And outside sudden rule changes. **[Learn how Americans are quietly reassessing their retirement—and how a gold IRA can help restore control while options still exist.](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)** [](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D) **FREE for a Limited Time!**

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

clean_body: Tesla’s latest self-driving footage just stunned everyone. Futuristic? Definitely. Impressive? No doubt. But around the 20-second mark... | | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | Tesla’s latest self-driving footage just stunned everyone. President Trump just approved two major mining projects that had been stalled for years — and the timing has financial analysts on edge.

These sites contain minerals tied to America's energy grid, AI infrastructure, EV production, and defense systems...

And one metal sits at the center of all of it:

**Silver.**

The same silver that:

* broke **$68 an ounce**
* surged **129% last year**
* and is now projected to push toward **$100+**

Silver isn't acting like a traditional precious metal anymore.

It's acting like a **strained industrial asset** — a metal America needs more of, faster than we can produce it.

So when a President fast-tracks mineral access at the exact moment silver demand is exploding and supply is tightening...

That's not "routine policy."

**It's a signal.**

And if you have savings or retirement accounts exposed to the stock market or a weakening dollar, that signal matters — because silver just outperformed the S&P;, bonds, and cash. If demand keeps accelerating, the next leg up could happen before most people understand why.

You deserve to see the bigger picture **before** the markets react.

Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

name: Elissa Slotkin
subject: Remembering my dad, Curt
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ELISSA SLOTKIN FOR MICHIGAN
body:
#

Last week, we lost my father, Curt Slotkin, to a two-year battle with cancer. He fought hard, but was ready to go, and we were with him at home when he passed away.
There is no single person in my life who taught me more about integrity and decency than my dad. And no one who shaped me more as a person. I wrote a piece about what he taught me a few years ago, **[which you can read here.](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/3994?t=1&akid=2406%2E135344%2EcnXPuQ)**
In a complicated world, my dad had four, uncomplicated loves: Family. Friends. Farm. Food. And the absolute peak of his happiness was when his four of his loves were together at the same time: grilling a great piece of meat for his family and friends at the farm. One of the great lessons I take from his life is to find your 3 or 4 loves and focus on them consistently and intensely. Do that and your life will be well-lived.
[](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/3991?t=2&akid=2406%2E135344%2EcnXPuQ)
Thank you for reading,
Elissa

PAID FOR BY ELISSA SLOTKIN FOR MICHIGAN
P.O. Box 4145
East Lansing, MI 48826
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can [ unsubscribe here](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=3&akid=2406%2E135344%2EcnXPuQ).

Elissa Slotkin served in the Central Intelligence Agency and the Department of Defense. Use of her job titles and photographs during service do not imply endorsement by the Central Intelligence Agency OR the Department of Defense.

Israel’s minister Amichai Chikli believes Australian leaders failed to act on early signs before [...] | Senate Democrats Push Shutdown Over ICE Actions Wyatt’s Take Democrats are threatening to shut down the government over ICE funding. TO: House Democratic Caucus
FROM: A grateful Democrat
Subject: Thank you! **_Dear House Democrats – you make me proud to be a Democrat._**

Your fight to IMPEACH Trump’s stooges Pete Hegseth and RFK Jr. is exactly what our country needs right now.

The American people are sick and tired of Hegseth and RFK Jr.’s inexperience, dangerous lies, and life-threatening policies!

**This news gives me hope that we WILL hold them accountable — and stand up to Trump’s authoritarianism once and for all.**

**I’m so grateful to have you leading the way!**

[**That’s why I’m signing this card and chipping in $3 to the Voter Protection Project to show my appreciation and to help House Democrats FLIP the few seats we need to take back the majority »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/101892?t=9&akid=22719%2E878179%2Es4KUMe) | [**Donate $3 to sign now »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/101892?t=10&akid=22719%2E878179%2Es4KUMe)

—The Voter Protection Project
878179
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=22719%2E878179%2Es4KUMe). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=22719%2E878179%2Es4KUMe).**

name: James Carville
subject: an ungodly amount of money
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY RAJA FOR ILLINOIS
body:
Believe me when I tell you
These emails are the best way for our campaign to stay in touch with supporters like you. If you'd prefer, you can [unsubscribe here](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/K-_-4uI0EkWyg9pVsZKBKQ2?unsubscribedata=ocoSoGFBZmeIpAQqJzCA%2BWac6Avp6ZMAvSf6dNR1%2BVGhsYoyj7s6ufs1EyvVCNiQyajQWbJXPDJDjiH7GqyHBepLtD4m78rPF5SQKaIcOxOfPg38jxmiMnDU5iQrDZ9SuZN6h3VSC32wmQ0f2HkztJFaYy3jxqm28yz7vrDhCLAmLKx2wJG%2BRAfa2zHEPKEtHotNoBgtTAHqfkB8FuoJryx4ykczekEfYv6boDJiDzqDSrWJJxcphY0Ep%2FgYlg4FB3iG7u7Upadh7jk2z0xqGP3FLWMzP5v%2Fa199IMT5cz2%2Bkgi%2Ft8%2FLbf%2F4aFOvTCnEqfsA%2BtSaDRb%2FLwAObqfhE2jmE5ntMrZCN9FCkv4OXHfrn9%2BlyJ%2FAkkn8S0XRhSkCnoUk5kTgUiKw7pN%2BsJwqZ75k8u7RVx0aVUgrns4pNG%2FPs5HhVPl6B9MeGcBufZ2q).
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124046252/597388743/-1443723481?amount=25&amounts=250,100,50,25,10,5&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2026.01.26_Sherrod-Carville_B1_F1_S1_C1_90CL-365DON_RS-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MzE2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2M2Q2OWIwMi1jMGZhLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDIyNDgyZDI3OWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=XbOTyFoxBeOYM1XEdlm38VXLuvTR2HPnis1xCHSxaKo=) Peter, it’s James Carville. name: Death of irony
subject: Trump says CA maps are racist and violate Voting Rights Act
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Colorado Draws the Line
body:
You couldn’t make this up if you tried.
| | | | | [](https://6s79z9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001qdinOf4e-YAMOyFzeeFYW0SJ8xlQt65uzmbqHuHDd0GX1pUtOzGFCv1J3fVmWowvA12H70_YjwQOJkTdjTYCG5Crov-ePpqkhHHJqXSK0ag0IgQ9i0gIG0w_-U9x6GlrLlpxtJfgx5h5sPZ3n4Qs6iNS8re2RTY6Yp2JnxyDKe1AYFH2PI0R03FElW8c5PpDHFA_pPbRt4OBMGeRWFgD4g==&c=Cjyu1I70q_An5uLacyxPkadiMmGDfzOPm4-8C5p6znLsaJv6uoWFog==&ch=4dkD7l1IW5MRRXzFpKn3EydhXmLDNT3d10RwVnVaOHUbBJ5ahnKHXQ==)
---
Peter,
As his approval rating continues to nosedive, Trump is getting desperate to save his master plan to stack the deck for MAGA this midterm election by gerrymandering red state districts.
Trump's Solicitor General asked the Supreme Court with a straight face (and zero evidence) to block California's new voter-approved maps, claiming they are racist and violate the Voting Rights Act--at the exact same time that the DOJ is working 24/7 to convince the Court to strike down the very same provisions of the Voting Rights Act.
---
| [](https://6s79z9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001qdinOf4e-YAMOyFzeeFYW0SJ8xlQt65uzmbqHuHDd0GX1pUtOzGFCv1J3fVmWowvA12H70_YjwQOJkTdjTYCG5Crov-ePpqkhHHJqXSK0ag0IgQ9i0gIG0w_-U9x6GlrLlpxtJfgx5h5sPZ3n4Qs6iNS8re2RTY6Yp2JnxyDKe1AYFH2PI0R03FElW8c5PpDcjHNZdeInDvsB_sA09s9eQ==&c=Cjyu1I70q_An5uLacyxPkadiMmGDfzOPm4-8C5p6znLsaJv6uoWFog==&ch=4dkD7l1IW5MRRXzFpKn3EydhXmLDNT3d10RwVnVaOHUbBJ5ahnKHXQ==)
---
Irony won't survive this one. The hypocrisy of the Trump Administration knows no boundaries.
But if the MAGA conservative Supreme Court decides to take the bait and accept the Trump Administration's nonsense arguments, the new California maps that wiped away 5 GOP seats won't survive either.
Accordingly, Democrats' fight to counter Trump's midterm election rigging will be dealt a critical blow.
Thankfully, Coloradans refuse to let Donald Trump destroy democracy and free and fair elections. Colorado Draws the Line is leading the counterattack: we're spearheading the statewide ballot initiative that would flip three or even four GOP-held seats in Colorado from red to blue.
As an added bonus, our campaign would very likely result in ultra-MAGA Lauren Boebert losing her seat in Congress for good, making her a victim of Trump's unquenchable thirst for power--a poetic end to her pathetic career.
[Trump is betting that we don't have the guts to pull this off. clean_body: You couldn’t make this up if you tried. | | | | | == Peter, As his approval rating continues to nosedive, Trump is getting desperate to save his master plan to stack the deck for MAGA this midterm election by gerrymandering red state districts. Trump's Solicitor General asked the Supreme Court with a straight face (and zero evidence) to block California's new voter-approved maps, claiming they are racist and violate the Voting Rights Act--at the exact same time that the DOJ is working 24/7 to convince the Court to strike down the very same provisions of the Voting Rights Act. | == Irony won't survive this one. The hypocrisy of the Trump Administration knows no boundaries. But if the MAGA conservative Supreme Court decides to take the bait and accept the Trump Administration's nonsense arguments, the new California maps that wiped away 5 GOP seats won't survive either. Accordingly, Democrats' fight to counter Trump's midterm election rigging will be dealt a critical blow. Thankfully, Coloradans refuse to let Donald Trump destroy democracy and free and fair elections. Colorado Draws the Line is leading the counterattack: we're spearheading the statewide ballot initiative that would flip three or even four GOP-held seats in Colorado from red to blue. As an added bonus, our campaign would very likely result in ultra-MAGA Lauren Boebert losing her seat in Congress for good, making her a victim of Trump's unquenchable thirst for power--a poetic end to her pathetic career. == If you've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly: | | == | == | == | | == | == | == | == Thank you for standing with us. subject: A Secret Service Agent Just Got Suspended Because of Us
body:
What I'm about to show you is one of the most alarming security breaches we've ever exposed. A U.S. Secret Service agent — assigned to protect
[View in Web Browser](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e1187967/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3YvN0Jja01WejRNZw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNjAxMjY/LbmNsNFpMUzc5YQ/qP2hpZHM9bmNFbThTalBEZm1lalBscw/gaXd4Zw/s8q75e672a4)

| | | | | [****](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
Dear Friend,

What I'm about to show you is one of the most alarming security breaches we've ever exposed.

A U.S. Secret Service agent — assigned to protect Vice President JD Vance — was caught on hidden camera **leaking sensitive security information** to one of our undercover journalists.

[**Watch the video — and please, help fund our next investigation.**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)

[****](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
His name is Tomas Escotto. A Biden-era holdover who admitted he voted for Joe Biden and said he "hates ICE."

And over the course of our investigation, he revealed:

* How the Vice President is physically surrounded by his protective detail
* Multiple daily shift changes and advance security procedures
* Future travel plans — sometimes days in advance
* He even sent images from inside Air Force Two

All of this to someone he thought was a _casual romantic interest._

[**You have to see this for yourself.**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)

**The result?** Within hours of our report, the Secret Service placed Escotto on administrative leave. His security clearance was suspended. His access to agency facilities and systems — revoked.

The Deputy Director of the Secret Service issued an internal memo to the entire agency acknowledging the breach — and apologized directly to the Vance family.

**This is what accountability looks like.**

Remember — this comes after someone already broke into JD Vance's Ohio home. The Vice President and his family have faced real security threats. And a Secret Service agent was casually handing out operational details to a stranger.

If our undercover journalist hadn't caught him, who else might have?

We worked closely with the Secret Service before publishing this story. We redacted sensitive details at their request. Our goal was never to endanger the Vice President — it was to expose a vulnerability that needed to be fixed.

**And now it's being fixed — because of your support.**

[**Will you help us keep going?**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)

Investigations like this take months of careful planning. Background checks. Legal review. Fieldwork. Training our citizen journalists in undercover techniques that can withstand scrutiny.

[**A gift of $35**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.) covers a background check. [**$120**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.) funds a full day of fieldwork. [**Any amount**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.) helps us train more citizen journalists and expose more corruption.

The mainstream media won't do this work. They'd rather protect the institutions than expose the rot inside them.

**That's why they need us. That's why they need you.**

[**Please donate today.**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)

Thank you for standing with us.

In Truth,
James O'Keefe
Founder and CEO
O'Keefe Media Group

---
[****](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---

This email was sent to **[dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com)** as part of your subscription to **America First War Room**

108 S Washington St. Suite 200
Alexandria, VA 22314

[Unsubscribe](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e1187967/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3N1YnNjcmlwdGlvbnMvdW5zdWJzY3JpYmU/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNjAxMjY/LbmNsNFpMUzc5YQ/qP2hpZHM9bmNFbThTalBEZm1lalBscw/gaXd4Zw/sek9cc2b4a6)

# [Greenland and the Price of American Power](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=185843735&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1ODQzNzM1LCJpYXQiOjE3Njk0Mzc4MDAsImV4cCI6MTc3MjAyOTgwMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.GNhH81Q33RPxiZYbZFEr3DpGZL3BA3W7hcAPyUk79S4)
### What Trump's territorial ambitions reveal about transactional diplomacy
| | [Patrick J. Murphy](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---
| Jan 26
---
| [](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=185843735&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1ODQzNzM1LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2OTQzNzgwMCwiZXhwIjoxNzcyMDI5ODAwLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDM5OTEzMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.3Z9wbK3L4wf7xesJoK4nQgh_QzIpMUVlhaWJenw79nU&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=185843735&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1ODQzNzM1LCJpYXQiOjE3Njk0Mzc4MDAsImV4cCI6MTc3MjAyOTgwMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.GNhH81Q33RPxiZYbZFEr3DpGZL3BA3W7hcAPyUk79S4&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=185843735&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1ODQzNzM1LCJpYXQiOjE3Njk0Mzc4MDAsImV4cCI6MTc3MjAyOTgwMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.GNhH81Q33RPxiZYbZFEr3DpGZL3BA3W7hcAPyUk79S4)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvcGF0cmlja211cnBoeXBhL3AvZ3JlZW5sYW5kLWFuZC10aGUtcHJpY2Utb2YtYW1lcmljYW4_.GB__EgC94gtCxYkeTXRkQrTXSWTOM1YhRmA5TOGVhFM?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/patrickmurphypa/p/greenland-and-the-price-of-american?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Since 2019, Donald Trump has floated the idea of purchasing Greenland from Denmark. For years, it was treated as something of a joke, another Trumpian provocation that sounded too implausible to take seriously.
Now, after Trump's explicit remarks at the [World Economic Forum](https://substack.com/redirect/216450b9-4732-4cf0-bab3-5700fb35c57e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in Davos, it's clear he's serious.
Standing before world leaders, Trump insisted that Greenland is a strategic necessity for missile defense, Arctic surveillance, and strategic depth in an era defined by long-range weapons and contested polar routes. Only the United States, he argued, is capable of securing it.
Had that been the full story, markets might have shrugged and moved on.
But then the president added something else. He reached back to World War II, arguing that Denmark had failed to defend Greenland at the time and that perhaps giving the territory back had been a mistake.
While Trump walked back taking Greenland militarily, he threatened punitive [tariffs](https://substack.com/redirect/118840a3-901f-40c3-a1c0-c8a0137b1046?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) against [European countries](https://substack.com/redirect/066bb42d-feba-4df9-ac98-577b67b16063?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that were set to begin Feb. 1, before changing course the following day, saying he's already formed ["the framework of a future deal"](https://substack.com/redirect/36e15c9c-7c5f-4095-bacd-57a5b56f4857?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) after meeting with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte who publicly praised Trump for forcing Europe and Canada to raise defense spending.
Others were less reassured. [U.S. senators, including Republicans](https://substack.com/redirect/e8d4315b-ae1a-4cad-8b8a-c8d4bc6b858b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), warned that Trump's posture risks alienating allies and would be a waste of taxpayer dollars.
Canada, meanwhile, found itself under Trump's harsh scrutiny. [Trump singled out Prime Minister Mark Carney](https://substack.com/redirect/8d0337a0-efe5-452d-ad73-2ee18b57ec26?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), accusing Canada of ingratitude and describing it as a security dependent of the United States. Carney called the moment a "rupture, not a transition." The old rules-based order, flawed as it was, at least offered open sea lanes, financial stability, and collective security. That bargain, he implied, is fraying.
The Secretary's SitRep is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..tHpASkNwrAtmLXWfeuc1kEwCwJ6HJQcOj3abOfsqZB4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=185843735)
None of this means the U.S. is about to lose its place at the center of the global system. But it does suggest that the premium attached to that position is no longer assumed.
Many world leaders are unsettled for a simple reason. The modern international order rests on a basic assumption: sovereign territory is not something powerful countries negotiate away from their allies. When the United States floats the idea of buying Greenland, it starts to sound like a world where raw power determines outcomes and alliances are purely transactional.
If territory can be pressured loose, then longstanding alliances themselves start to look conditional.
That is alarming not just for Denmark, but for any small or mid-sized country whose security depends on diplomatic norms rather than leverage.
It also begs the question: what problem is the U.S. trying to solve that isn't already solved?
The United States already possesses everything Trump claims to need. Through a 1951 defense agreement with Denmark, America operates Pituffik Space Base, a permanent installation supporting missile warning, space surveillance, and Arctic monitoring. Roughly 150 U.S. service members are stationed there with full Danish cooperation in an area with only 56K people.
In other words, the security access Trump cites as justification already exists.
If access is already guaranteed, why press for control?
Absorbing Greenland would make the United States the second-largest country in the world by land area and mark the largest territorial expansion in American history, [surpassing the Louisiana Purchase](https://substack.com/redirect/4bd16595-0fd6-4595-a059-b070e8df66a5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). On paper, it would look like a dramatic assertion of power. In practice, it would add little that the United States does not already have in terms of security access or military reach.
If America acquired Greenland, it would test how far pressure-based diplomacy can go before it begins to unsettle the confidence it depends on.
And confidence, once shaken, does not immediately return.
Much of the immediate commentary focused on whether this was merely a negotiating tactic, or simply another example of Trump being Trump. [Investors were adjusting their positions](https://substack.com/redirect/93baa0b1-be0b-4003-835c-1edc131bcb72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) based on this volatility.
For years, markets have learned to interpret Trump's opening positions as maximalist bids designed to create leverage. The working assumption has been that they soften with time. Wall Street even has a name for this: [TACO](https://substack.com/redirect/3de8fd0b-a18d-4380-83dd-16ce4639d16e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--Trump Always Chickens Out. I don't write this gleefully when talking about our President.
Before markets rebounded, though, they took a hard hit. European equities sold off across the board.
More notably, though, the [dollar fell nearly 1%](https://substack.com/redirect/d4512105-da03-410e-849c-dc7aeb64a49c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in a single session--the steepest drop since the spring tariff rollout after a year in which the dollar dropped 10% while the Euro and Russian Ruble both went up double digits. Treasury prices fell too, pushing yields higher. Stocks can sell off for a dozen reasons, but currencies and sovereign debt tend to move only when confidence is being reassessed.
By Tuesday morning, strategists stopped calling it "risk-off" and started calling it "[Sell America](https://substack.com/redirect/de45d65d-cc1e-4301-8489-026c3def5575?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)."
Investors are rethinking whether U.S. assets deserve the automatic premium they've enjoyed for decades.
At the same time, when Trump softened his tone and paused tariffs, markets rallied sharply. The Dow [rose 550 points](https://substack.com/redirect/744c3305-8a81-444b-a45b-5bc63f8402da?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). The [S&P 500](https://substack.com/redirect/3a8b945c-0f33-4223-b564-d34a1636ce4c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and Nasdaq followed.
Wall Street believes TACO appeared to be back.
Large banks started assigning probabilities. [JPMorgan](https://substack.com/redirect/5e493a50-70b0-4e76-9b87-f65f3f696cd8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) estimated a 55% chance of a negotiated outcome that expands U.S. access without a formal transfer of sovereignty. They put the odds of tariffs being fully implemented at closer to 40%.
And then there's the Supreme Court.
Analysts began to price in a [roughly 50% chance](https://substack.com/redirect/ad851e4e-4452-490c-bd2f-69122b51e7f4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that the Court could strike down Trump's tariff authority under IEEPA. We might hear as early as this week on a Supreme Court ruling, which has a 6-3 Republican edge.
In that sense, Greenland is no longer just a territorial question. It has become a stress test of alliances, of markets, and of how much pressure the system can absorb before confidence begins to fray. Make no mistake, this is a game of chess, not checkers, and as we used to say in the Army - loyalty above all, except honor - we all need to be loyal to our American values, not our political parties.

[](https://substack.com/redirect/993beddd-5c33-4d27-af75-aaf1629bd1f4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
**Recommendation - SUPPORT**
**SB2284:** This bill expands the legality of on-farm raw milk by allowing incidental sales from small herds of cows, goats, or sheep and clarifying that owners may use and sell raw milk from their animals for personal and non-institutional use.
### **Full Analysis**
_A vote for this bill is a vote to expand food freedom and small-farm commerce by legalizing more direct raw milk sales to informed consumers and limiting state barriers to private dairy transactions._
#### **Key Provisions & Tradeoffs**
* Extends existing incidental raw goat milk provisions to include cows and sheep, capped at nine producing animals on the premises.
* Allows raw milk sales directly from farms to consumers (not for institutional use) and clarifies that independent or partial owners may use raw milk for personal consumption or other personal use.
* Requires non-advertised raw milk sales and adequate notice to consumers of the inherent risks of unprocessed dairy, while leaving the broader regulatory and fee structure for processed dairy intact.

#### **Liberty Analysis**
From a liberty and economic perspective, this bill is a meaningful step toward food freedom and respect for private property and contract. Current law allows only very limited incidental sales of raw goat milk. SB2284 broadens that to cows and sheep, clarifies that owners (including partial/"cow share" style owners) may consume and use raw milk from their own animals, and allows direct farm-to-consumer raw milk sales so long as they are not for institutional use. These changes reduce the criminalization of voluntary exchange between small farmers and informed consumers, and they honor the principle that individuals should be free to decide what they eat and drink without heavy-handed state interference.

Economically, the bill opens up new, low-capital opportunities for small farms without any new subsidies, tax expenditures, or regulatory agencies. It does not increase fees or spending and leaves existing Grade A/processors' regulatory framework largely untouched. There are still constraints that are not fully liberty-aligned: the no-advertising rule for raw milk sales, the cap of nine producing animals, and the continuing misdemeanor framework for unauthorized unpasteurized milk outside the bill's carve-outs. However, relative to current law, this is a clear net deregulation that decentralizes food production, increases consumer choice, and slightly reduces the scope of criminal law in peaceful agricultural commerce.

From a civil-liberties and sound-money perspective, the bill does not expand enforcement powers, fines, or state surveillance, and it does not rely on borrowing, subsidies, or central economic planning. The requirement for "adequate notice" of health risks is a light-touch consumer information rule, not a mandate to alter product composition or submit to additional inspections. While a purist libertarian might prefer full repeal of raw milk restrictions, this bill moves Mississippi policy in the right direction--toward limited government, voluntary exchange, and stronger property rights for small farmers and consumers.

clean_body: Mississippi Senate Bill - 2284 | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? from the desk ofDana Criswell Mississippi Senate Bill - 2284 | | | Jan 26 | | | | | | | | | | | | Recommendation - SUPPORT SB2284: This bill expands the legality of on-farm raw milk by allowing incidental sales from small herds of cows, goats, or sheep and clarifying that owners may use and sell raw milk from their animals for personal and non-institutional use. Full Analysis A vote for this bill is a vote to expand food freedom and small-farm commerce by legalizing more direct raw milk sales to informed consumers and limiting state barriers to private dairy transactions. Key Provisions & Tradeoffs Extends existing incidental raw goat milk provisions to include cows and sheep, capped at nine producing animals on the premises. Allows raw milk sales directly from farms to consumers (not for institutional use) and clarifies that independent or partial owners may use raw milk for personal consumption or other personal use. Requires non-advertised raw milk sales and adequate notice to consumers of the inherent risks of unprocessed dairy, while leaving the broader regulatory and fee structure for processed dairy intact. Liberty Analysis From a liberty and economic perspective, this bill is a meaningful step toward food freedom and respect for private property and contract. Current law allows only very limited incidental sales of raw goat milk. SB2284 broadens that to cows and sheep, clarifies that owners (including partial/"cow share" style owners) may consume and use raw milk from their own animals, and allows direct farm-to-consumer raw milk sales so long as they are not for institutional use. These changes reduce the criminalization of voluntary exchange between small farmers and informed consumers, and they honor the principle that individuals should be free to decide what they eat and drink without heavy-handed state interference. Economically, the bill opens up new, low-capital opportunities for small farms without any new subsidies, tax expenditures, or regulatory agencies. It does not increase fees or spending and leaves existing Grade A/processors' regulatory framework largely untouched. There are still constraints that are not fully liberty-aligned: the no-advertising rule for raw milk sales, the cap of nine producing animals, and the continuing misdemeanor framework for unauthorized unpasteurized milk outside the bill's carve-outs. However, relative to current law, this is a clear net deregulation that decentralizes food production, increases consumer choice, and slightly reduces the scope of criminal law in peaceful agricultural commerce. From a civil-liberties and sound-money perspective, the bill does not expand enforcement powers, fines, or state surveillance, and it does not rely on borrowing, subsidies, or central economic planning. The requirement for "adequate notice" of health risks is a light-touch consumer information rule, not a mandate to alter product composition or submit to additional inspections. While a purist libertarian might prefer full repeal of raw milk restrictions, this bill moves Mississippi policy in the right direction--toward limited government, voluntary exchange, and stronger property rights for small farmers and consumers. Read all of Dana 's post and stay informed about politics in Mississippi** | | | | | | | (C) 2026 Dana Criswell Mississippi ? ?
